clean_body: - Michele Tafoya here! I never really considered running for office. Ever. I was too focused on raising my family, and being the best football sideline reporter I could be. But even in the last few weeks, Minnesota has become the embodiment of everything wrong with politics today. Tim Walz, his inner circle, and the corrupt political class are too busy: Wasting billions of our tax dollars on fraudulent daycares Failing to condemn the activists storming churches Raising prices on hardworking families Sabotaging anything the President wants to do Forcing men into women's spaces Pitting Americans against each other I can 't sit on the sidelines anymore. That's why I'm throwing my hat in the ring and announcing my candidacy for U.S. Senate! thought long and hard about the dynamics of this race before jumping in. Minnesota has long been considered simply out of reach for Republicans. The last time it voted for a GOP President was Nixon. The pundits and political experts say a Republican could never win here. But this year is going to be different for three key reasons: FIRST - I'm running against Tim Walz's right hand. All the rampant fraud that took place here happened under Peggy Flanagan's nose as much as it did under his. We will remind voters about that fact every single day. SECOND - The President has increased his voter share each time he has run. He nearly flipped this state in the last election, and Kamala Harris' running mate was our sitting Governor! Momentum is on our side. THIRD - I'm a different kind of candidate, and I know how to win. I didn't reach the top of my industry by pulling my punches. I put in the work. I never backed down. And I kept fighting, no matter what the naysayers said. In my years on the sidelines, I 've seen a LOT of underdogs win football games that the "experts" said were impossible. The Minnesota Senate race will be another chapter in that storied history. | | ---|--- Thanks in advance for your support. I'll never forget the good folks who stepped up in the opening days of this campaign. You and I are going to do great things together. I can feel it. Be brave. Do good. - Michele Michele Tafoya Republican for Senate, Minnesota It's official - Michele Tafoya is running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota! Endorse her here:** | Paid for by Tafoya for Senate 3500 Vicksburg LN N, STE 267, Plymouth, MN 55447 |

name: Brad for Georgia
subject: The Raff Report - No Games
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BRAD FOR GOVERNOR, INC
body:
| | | | | [|
---](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=5cc977e499&e=6036811227)
| | | | | | Friends,
Earlier this week, Lt. Governor Burt Jones lashed out at Brad for refusing to break the law and release the Social Security numbers of millions of Georgians to unnamed third-party entities.

Apparently the Lt. Gov. missed Brad’s [Op-Ed](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=d53861d695&e=6036811227) where he was unequivocal: Georgia law matters, and so does protecting the personal information of all our citizens.

Rest assured, we won’t be distracted by the attacks. While others chase clicks, we’re focused on what really matters: advancing policies that make Georgia more affordable and safe while building a grassroots movement strong enough to deliver a decisive victory at the ballot box.

We are standing with Georgia—no matter what.

-Team Raff
---
[|
---](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=133acc0f65&e=6036811227)
| |

This week, Brad called on the Georgia General Assembly to pass new policies that require REAL ID to cast a ballot in state elections. These policies, which should be part of Georgia law, solidify our state’s position as a national leader in election security.

_“Our state has set the standard for election integrity through strong voter ID laws, accurate voter rolls, and transparent post-election audits. We have defended voter ID requirements, citizenship verification, and list maintenance, including standing up to pressure from Stacey Abrams and national activist groups, because these common sense policies are lawful and backed by Georgia voters.”

“As technology and threats evolve, so must our safeguards. REAL ID is the gold standard for identification in America today. If it is required to board an airplane or enter a federal building, it should also be required to choose our leaders at the local, state, and federal level.”

_REAL ID is a federal identification standard established by Congress through the REAL ID Act of 2005 in response to the 9/11 Commission’s recommendations. The law sets higher security standards for state-issued driver’s licenses and identification cards.

_“Georgia’s election system is secure. Let’s keep it that way. I urge the Georgia House and Senate to pass legislation requiring REAL ID for voting. REAL ID protects lawful voters, reinforces trust in election outcomes, and ensures Georgia elections remain the best in America.”_
---
[|
---](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=667fdbf137&e=6036811227)
| | | | | | | | | | | | **WSB-TV Exclusive: Georgia Secretary of State wants state to pass bill requiring Real ID to vote

** Brad sat down with WSB-TV’s Richard Elliot to discuss continuing Georgia's position as the leader in election security and integrity.

_“Real ID is the ‘gold standard’ for identification. In fact, if you want to go on an airplane, you want to get into a federal office building, you have to have Real ID.

“We do a great job in Georgia, and that’s why we’re recognized for having the most secure, safe election in the entire country by many organizations. But the ‘gold standard’ is having Real ID. So I’m going to call on the General Assembly to pass Real ID this session.”

_Watch the entire interview [**HERE**](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=a740444d26&e=6036811227).
---
| | **Griffen Daily News: Secretary of State Raffensperger visits Griffin; shares goals in campaign for governor

** While speaking to the Griffen Exchange Club, Brad layed out his plan for a more affordable and safe further for Georgia with lower property taxes and bringing better paying jobs back to the Peach State.

_“One of the things that I’ve seen is, when I travel across Georgia, no matter where it is, it’s become abundantly clear that Georgians are worried,” Raffensperger said.

“What they’re worried about is that no matter how hard they work, no matter how many hours they put in the day, that it’s just not enough.”

_Great-paying jobs, he said, are the best way to improve affordability in the state. If elected, he said he will work to bring more high-paying jobs to Georgia, citing his experience as a businessman.
Read the entire article [**HERE**](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=2a0cafd85b&e=6036811227).
---
|
---
| |

On the campaign trail this week, Brad met with the Georgia Alliance of School Resource Officers and Educators at their Executive Seminar in Columbus and the Exchange Club in Griffin. He discussed his plan to relieve Georgians of burdensome property taxes and bring more good-paying jobs to communities across the state.
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | | | | |

Brad is leading a movement to bring jobs back to Georgia, cap property taxes, and keep our families safe from harm.

To win, we need committed supporters willing to lend their time and resources to build something truly great.

Joining Team Raff means helping spread the message, supporting local efforts, and standing up for hardworking Georgians across the state.

Add your name and become part of the team [**HERE**](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=e0008b77ec&e=6036811227).

Together, we will make Georgia affordable and safe!
---
|
---
| |

Don’t forget to follow Brad on social media for real-time updates!
---

name: Ilhan Omar Alerts via PTV

Fellow Patriot,
---
Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal.

It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war.

His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before...

**It was about the Sabbath. **

About sacred rest. Reconnecting with God.

Charlie wrote this book because he knew what America was missing... and what he nearly lost himself.

He found that in a world addicted to busyness, noise, and scrolling...

_**The most radical act of resistance is to STOP. **_

To rest. To reflect. To return to God.

This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation.

🔥 You'll discover how Charlie learned to honor the Sabbath in a culture that never stops

🔥 How setting aside just one day a week gave him clarity, peace, and strength

🔥 Why he believed this message could spark a spiritual revival across America

I know he would've wanted you to have this book.

**When you give today to Turning Point USA, you 're not just supporting the movement Charlie built -- you're helping pass on the message he felt most urgently in his final months. **

clean_body: One day a week. A radical act of trust. Charlie Kirk’s final book on Sabbath rest—make a gift to TPUSA and we’ll send you a copy. | | Fellow Patriot, Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal. It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war. His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before... It was about the Sabbath. About sacred rest. Reconnecting with God. Charlie wrote this book because he knew what America was missing... and what he nearly lost himself. He found that in a world addicted to busyness, noise, and scrolling... The most radical act of resistance is to STOP. To rest. To reflect. To return to God. This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation. 🔥 You'll discover how Charlie learned to honor the Sabbath in a culture that never stops 🔥 How setting aside just one day a week gave him clarity, peace, and strength 🔥 Why he believed this message could spark a spiritual revival across America I know he would've wanted you to have this book. When you give today to Turning Point USA, you 're not just supporting the movement Charlie built -- you're helping pass on the message he felt most urgently in his final months. Fellow Patriot, thank you, in advance, for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage -- the very things Charlie gave his life for. And as you do, may the message of this book lead you -- just as it led Charlie -- to a deeper trust in God, a stronger love for your family, and a renewed peace in your own soul. For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating Officer Turning Point USA P.S. Charlie Kirk's final book, Stop, in the Name of God, is a heartfelt call to slow down, remember what's eternal, and rest in God. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

name: Demand Justice PAC
subject: This isn’t normal. It’s a takeover:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260125-61110&t=1001&akid=61110%2E1407376%2E0UdoeR ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260125-61110&t=1002&akid=61110%2E1407376%2E0UdoeR ]Demand Justice PAC

Trump is racing to seize control of our courts — stacking them with judges
loyal to him, not the Constitution, and eager to protect billionaire power
while working people pay the price.

These are lifetime appointments. If we don’t stop this now, his grip on
our rights and our democracy will last for decades.

Demand Justice is not staying quiet. We’re going on offense — calling out
the politicians who enable this takeover, exposing the corruption baked
into Trump’s judicial picks, and turning nationwide outrage into real,
undeniable pressure.

Here’s how we’re making it happen:

🔥 No Free Passes. Period: Republican or Democrat — if lawmakers refuse to
stand up to Trump’s court takeover, we make sure they answer for it. We
apply pressure early, publicly, and relentlessly every step of the way.

🔎 Exposing the Court Capture Scheme: Trump’s nominees aren’t neutral
judges. They’re handpicked operatives who rig the system for Trump, his
allies, and the billionaire class. Demand Justice pulls back the curtain —
naming names and showing exactly how these judges threaten our rights.

✊ Building People Power at Scale: The courts decide everything:
healthcare, abortion access, voting rights, worker protections. Your
support fuels a national movement to block dangerous nominees, organize
grassroots pressure, and win fights that actually matter.

We don’t have the luxury of waiting. Every judge Trump installs today
makes it harder to defend our rights tomorrow.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260125.YvhKhn&refcode=em20260125-61110&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61110%2E1407376%2E0UdoeR ]If you’re ready to fight back, chip in $5 right now to
hold politicians accountable, expose judicial corruption, and mobilize
grassroots power to take back the courts.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260125.YvhKhn&amount=5&refcode=em20260125-61110&t=1022&akid=61110%2E1407376%2E0UdoeR ]Donate $5 ››

The courts shouldn’t serve Trump. They shouldn’t serve billionaires. They
should serve the people — and with your help, we’ll make sure they do.

Thank you,

Demand Justice PAC

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.demandjustice.org/survey/less-emails?t=1023&akid=61110%2E1407376%2E0UdoeR
Update Your Information: https://act.demandjustice.org/signup/update-your-information/?t=1024&akid=61110%2E1407376%2E0UdoeR
Unsubscribe: https://act.demandjustice.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?email=%%email%%&t=1025&akid=61110%2E1407376%2E0UdoeR
Email us: feedback@demandjustice.org

Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE, #192, Washington DC 20003
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Contributions or gifts to Demand Justice PAC are not tax deductible.

name: GIFFORDS.org
subject: America has a problem.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
#

Team,
We are only three weeks into 2026, and the statistics from the Gun Violence Archive are disturbing:
**
* 740 gun involved deaths
* 1300 gun involved injuries
* 18 mass shootings
* 40 children killed or injured
* 190 teenagers killed or injured
**

These numbers will only continue to grow. This is not normal. This is not okay.
We have witnessed tragedy after tragedy, and yet too many lawmakers STILL refuse to acknowledge our gun violence crisis — much less address it.
But for the families affected — who wait for hours on end at the emergency room, who will have an empty seat at the dinner table for the rest of their lives — they can never, ever forget.
They deserve more than thoughts and prayers.
Gun violence is a uniquely American problem. We can’t give up in the fight to keep our country safe. While we have made progress in the movement to end gun violence, we still live in a nation where the threat of gun violence looms over us daily.
We must pass universal background checks at the federal level. We must make sure guns are safely stored. We must vote out extremist elected officials who put the gun lobby’s radical agenda above the safety of the people they are supposed to serve.
That is the mission we have at GIFFORDS.
**[So please, can you make a contribution of $5 to GIFFORDS PAC today? We need you with us in the fight to end gun violence in our country.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-q12026.IDrYd1&refcode=em260125-1-b&t=1&akid=11204%2E3935704%2EOPt1Ja)

name: Abdul El-Sayed
subject: I’d be the first public health official to ever serve in the United States Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1604%2E251025%2EfyEDlz ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1604%2E251025%2EfyEDlz ]Abdul for US Senate

Peter,

I’m a doctor, and if I’m elected, I’d be the first public health official
to ever serve in the United States Senate.

That shouldn’t be remarkable. But in a MAGA-majority Congress, it is.

Right now, healthcare isn’t being treated like a human right. It’s being
treated like a bargaining chip.

MAGA politicians are cutting healthcare funding to funnel even more money
to ICE, so they can continue attacking innocent people and terrorizing our
communities.

We’re watching politicians with no medical background, no public health
training, and no respect for science — from RFK Jr. on down — make
decisions that hurt real people.

It doesn’t take a medical degree to tell you this is insane.

Access to healthcare shouldn’t be up for debate,
Peter. That’s why I support
Medicare for All, and why I’ve spent my career working to ease the burden
our current broken system puts on working people.

I’m ready to take this fight to the next level, but to do that, I need
your support.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20260125-1-ak.5OUT5M&amount=10&refcode=em20260125-1599&t=1004&akid=1604%2E251025%2EfyEDlz ]If you’re able, will you chip in $10, $25, or whatever you can today?

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20260125-1-ak.5OUT5M&amount=10&refcode=em20260125-1599&t=1023&akid=1604%2E251025%2EfyEDlz ]Donate $10 ››

I’ve never taken a dime from Big Pharma. I’m the only candidate in this
race who has never accepted money from corporate PACs — and never will.

That means if we’re going to win and make real, historic progress for
working people, it will be because of grassroots
contributors.

In solidarity,

Abdul

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. To contribute via check, please address it to Abdul for U.S. Senate, PO Box 126, St. Clair Shores, MI 48080.

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1024&akid=1604%2E251025%2EfyEDlz
Update Your Information: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/signup/update-your-information
Unsubscribe: https://act.abdulformichigan.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1025&akid=1604%2E251025%2EfyEDlz

Email us: info@abdulformichigan.com

Paid for by Abdul for U.S. Senate

name: Flash poll via StateDemocrats.com
subject: How will you resist the Trump administration in 2026?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
We want to hear from voters like you, Peter. Please respond before midnight »
#

**How will you resist the Trump administration in 2026, Peter?** As Trump’s GOP continues to attack our rights + freedoms, we want to know how everyday people like you are committed to resisting – in ways big and small – over this next year. **Please read just a few options below, then confirm your selections by clicking the button and submitting the survey:** I will vote
I will attend protests + rallies
I will call my elected officials
I will pay attention to the news
I will stay engaged in my community
I will participate in boycotts
I will take care of myself + my loved ones

name: Jeremiah Galus
subject: Chicago Schools Are Excluding Christians
body:
Chicago Public Schools desperately needs qualified teachers.
The fight to keep men out of women's sports isn't over.
| | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/u/qTM37jhSi-wjSyA4NfLMRLD9JmgkjsupXEeqr21zAbi-AZH139Cbd9_TbiAar0sgOjDd5cbJkMD4Qjgj8K9O5iKemeMyT_TPCcNCoCmO5-0Huh5Up6AgbGvCsEuVQM4-zSCkGMfdKaxMm6Qslhx3d0CUdB6HLuLZVf-WRFlRdAT0-e2E99J6Ny-x/i1ufWEx2M41oRvLZiqVajFnQ9V_6BP5u/17)_

**[Chicago Public Schools Discriminates Against Moody Bible Institute](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/tTWbhl612CrsKaXGA2XKc-SKelnSPt2DSecXbAe7Gmz6CpxGZHZdXoHqBBc-zJcGKQExcIAErSlSQHTlh62zoU3_ZIxGmhBTo9Q9Be7_FpiOLbqo73RTQThStYgE4OrzAX2hX1psgFLGHNSMf46PSmuIvn1LwVC0qqS0qnpoJp9t2q5-X8o6uFEMmxYOJnoO6_4GocP8Mzp5csSjlwTY4x5QsHlyk8pJPL422ujJZzmtNt-ygiV_mrYNia6B3Mt_0CybKLztwlfedfwYi090g9ZPgH8TLVy03tIfwtUcU9-722ntH9UaTUdSEEfwUhOPylqCH9uXx9vOgVW6bHzgDnOOJFsh9zzAtx4XwxrXcoiPcv9Hdn7alHAyYqiu6-j8UpnrRm0-9JOvk-4DvlDA1d4pRJ2MaYMbmtBs0qFzxhnSNL_B7tZQm0zJoYVNLcJBCcufN1sCIdEC8stLDYqgAy0CMl-0szKVSEKRp3ueSBmS-KNqPsjjsBBwtMlu_HwXX-YU5xJJ3ck-kT5837GfZrd6zh5aWiOVk0bZd4Ea2D0txeWsLqy9TDVcyKFqqpepmiYoFe-ZIOUjhSsVvSgOBlIkRJuzSph0le-vOy0BmyHdIr1bGcv4pBcgAIGklWc6ZdnmmPgRsWwQvMVxhp4YyqMUTOrDfRUpFoMRMlF_hRmM5-rn1aWLyThjPWXQ2hIhszVnYB6qez1oFte_i1uAS1x3dNEBeqUGl0fY/oEBD720kaycnye1C2uhOBxbExnl0jie5/17)******
Dear Fellow American,

Chicago Public Schools desperately needs qualified teachers. But school officials are choosing to discriminate against Christian student teachers rather than fill those vacancies.

The students at Moody Bible Institute are experiencing this discrimination firsthand.

Moody's students are barred from participating in Chicago Public School's student-teaching program. Not because they lack qualifications. Not because they lack passion for serving students. But because Moody exclusively hires staff who affirm and live out its biblical beliefs.

**In other words, Chicago Public Schools is punishing Moody students for attending a college that practices its faith.**

This is an attack on religious freedom--one of the foundational freedoms that has shaped our nation since its inception. Religious schools like Moody Bible Institute have the constitutional right to exclusively hire employees who share their faith and uphold their religious mission. Without this right, religious institutions would lose their very identity and purpose.

Moody's mission is clear: proclaim the gospel and equip people to be biblically grounded, practically trained, and to engage the world through gospel-centered living. For that mission to be credible, its faculty and staff must be unified in both belief and practice.

But when Moody requested a simple religious exemption acknowledging its constitutionally protected rights, the district refused.

**Instead, officials doubled down --effectively telling Moody students that their faith disqualifies them from serving Chicago's public school children.**

Alliance Defending Freedom is representing Moody Bible Institute in a federal lawsuit to challenge this discriminatory exclusion.

**Will you sign our Statement of Support for Moody Bible Institute and stand with them in this critical moment?
**
This isn't an isolated situation. We are watching this pattern repeat nationwide.

In Washington State, **Yakima Union Gospel Mission** serves thousands of homeless individuals every day--offering food, shelter, and the hope of Christ to locals who desperately need it. Yet the state is threatening the Mission because it only hires those who share its Christian beliefs. Now the Mission is in court seeking to protect its right to hire those who share and live out its faith.

Similarly, in Oregon, Youth 71Five Ministries is asking an appeals court to protect its right to receive public benefits without having to give up its right to employ those who share its faith. Youth 71Five Ministries serves at-risk youth, empowering them with the hope of the Gospel. It depends on a staff that shares its biblical beliefs. But officials stripped the ministry of its public funding for only hiring like-minded individuals who ensure that mission goes forward, violating its constitutional freedoms.

When ministries like Moody, Yakima Union Gospel Mission, and Youth 71Five Ministries stand up in court, they are not only protecting their own freedom. They are defending religious freedom and free expression for every American--Christian or not.

**But these battles come with real emotional and personal costs.** Leaders and staff can face hostility, intense public scrutiny, and even open threats. Stand alongside thousands of believers who are praying for Moody during these stressful times.

That's why your voice matters so deeply right now.

**Please take a moment to sign the Statement of Support today.**
[ SIGN THE STATEMENT OF SUPPORT](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/TaPH4tZ5pT2NpkbLcQzRiDuZsx6EbAkG1a1pt6YgfKlB0xn4jasoeY_wo_GgIsN42Ba-bBEg89gzUFCP0B2qWk3w3V8N8qz0PltlbfS_8PS_c_qpZhRVkh-E9sqz1i63ZJRJlhf_w9qIccvAe440oOZOLNriM1EeZbs3by42Mp3ZuncroaRS5dXxWbTHH7TvAMKQVL1bfkIoxVpXfJxQpM8X3mpKGZHwj5KVE7Gg5ha-FhDeXtkqrPOW-JZBzlJBy3ac43sz9KeJ3_KeoCkx5sUZcNrkGTN6p71-g5fz3kdr1v1mgzxX46x2S6gbFErZ0y5xDMywZOxyIxE_XTMN363pgNpAnTSN4reJpF-2Qy6VIDu5lD_5ElysCSaTjRRqjwn4xBD5LdlQx89WApNH0wGjmH48F8-L69LUgr3oKzxZBQsBOI_2pK8W68PCz2ygOfLXg6rjZq7w-JItp0ZArUyd6o_SGLBPoBfQPoVrP4GXUyOcKGOxtIrkud4Q-bg74_pr55ZlG--JCiKbMbHPBUFhntkRXfhufSmectDdLN8nnB2YKyIwxwJo1euM2sSjLCiFI61_NqDsWb2CejmTiUdeU6EHsxp1FEB68BJvBJKZf7osnmDBiYj2ZisH7ni4s2x-FHml2mnqgmaL0cm_rtqIjlsHDRUyqEzCUlp1pJuzop6pshgbTBx86BJ2DdCLMsdOdWLutSLkK82r1iHDqanFeE6btcno5xzj/ObMCgI-_VCrC4e1foTzxMwG9OKDD4GAX/17)

Thank you for standing with those who are standing for truth.
For freedom,

**Jeremiah Galus**
Senior Counsel
Alliance Defending Freedom

clean_body: Chicago Public Schools desperately needs qualified teachers. The fight to keep men out of women's sports isn't over. | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this | | | ** Dear Fellow American, Chicago Public Schools desperately needs qualified teachers. But school officials are choosing to discriminate against Christian student teachers rather than fill those vacancies. The students at Moody Bible Institute are experiencing this discrimination firsthand. Moody's students are barred from participating in Chicago Public School's student-teaching program. Not because they lack qualifications. Not because they lack passion for serving students. But because Moody exclusively hires staff who affirm and live out its biblical beliefs. In other words, Chicago Public Schools is punishing Moody students for attending a college that practices its faith. This is an attack on religious freedom--one of the foundational freedoms that has shaped our nation since its inception. Religious schools like Moody Bible Institute have the constitutional right to exclusively hire employees who share their faith and uphold their religious mission. Without this right, religious institutions would lose their very identity and purpose. Moody's mission is clear: proclaim the gospel and equip people to be biblically grounded, practically trained, and to engage the world through gospel-centered living. For that mission to be credible, its faculty and staff must be unified in both belief and practice. But when Moody requested a simple religious exemption acknowledging its constitutionally protected rights, the district refused. Instead, officials doubled down --effectively telling Moody students that their faith disqualifies them from serving Chicago's public school children. Alliance Defending Freedom is representing Moody Bible Institute in a federal lawsuit to challenge this discriminatory exclusion. Will you sign our Statement of Support for Moody Bible Institute and stand with them in this critical moment? This isn't an isolated situation. We are watching this pattern repeat nationwide. In Washington State, Yakima Union Gospel Mission serves thousands of homeless individuals every day--offering food, shelter, and the hope of Christ to locals who desperately need it. Yet the state is threatening the Mission because it only hires those who share its Christian beliefs. Now the Mission is in court seeking to protect its right to hire those who share and live out its faith. Similarly, in Oregon, Youth 71Five Ministries is asking an appeals court to protect its right to receive public benefits without having to give up its right to employ those who share its faith. Youth 71Five Ministries serves at-risk youth, empowering them with the hope of the Gospel. It depends on a staff that shares its biblical beliefs. But officials stripped the ministry of its public funding for only hiring like-minded individuals who ensure that mission goes forward, violating its constitutional freedoms. When ministries like Moody, Yakima Union Gospel Mission, and Youth 71Five Ministries stand up in court, they are not only protecting their own freedom. They are defending religious freedom and free expression for every American--Christian or not. But these battles come with real emotional and personal costs. Leaders and staff can face hostility, intense public scrutiny, and even open threats. Stand alongside thousands of believers who are praying for Moody during these stressful times. That's why your voice matters so deeply right now. Please take a moment to sign the Statement of Support today. Thank you for standing with those who are standing for truth. For freedom, Jeremiah Galus** Senior Counsel Alliance Defending Freedom Alliance Defending Freedom 15100 N. 90th Street, Scottsdale, Arizona 85260

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124014594/597278576/-1199461416?amount=10&source=DPG_EM_FR_01252026_B1_CROSSWELLSPLIT_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMGNkZTg2NS0wZGZhLWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDIyNDgyZDI3OWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6ZsFgWYSLOc2-It4d1VTdNrXu1ht1YeARivx_SLcwXs=) Peter, **Donald Trump just accepted a $2.5 million bribe to overturn my work as a federal prosecutor.** As a federal prosecutor, I led the investigation into Julio Herrera Velutini’s illegal bribery of the former Governor of Puerto Rico. Then, just as Herrera Velutini was at his criminal trial, his daughter made a $2.5 million contribution to MAGA Inc., Donald Trump’s super PAC. On Friday, Trump pardoned both the former governor and Herrera Velutini. This is the cycle of the Trump administration’s corruption: criminal cronyism, a donation, a pardon, and all of it completely out in the open. **This open sale of justice occurred SEPARATELY from the Eric Adams case, where the Trump administration asked me to drop a case for political reasons. I refused, because my oath was to the Constitution and the rule of law, not the President.** I have trained prosecutors worldwide on prosecuting corrupt public officials. And I can tell you that it doesn’t take long before corruption becomes a part of a nation’s culture. We are running out of time to stop the damage being done. That’s why I’m running for Congress — to fight for a justice system that serves the public, not the powerful. [To end this corruption, we must flip PA-07. clean_body: If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = = Peter, Donald Trump just accepted a $2.5 million bribe to overturn my work as a federal prosecutor. As a federal prosecutor, I led the investigation into Julio Herrera Velutini’s illegal bribery of the former Governor of Puerto Rico. Then, just as Herrera Velutini was at his criminal trial, his daughter made a $2.5 million contribution to MAGA Inc., Donald Trump’s super PAC. On Friday, Trump pardoned both the former governor and Herrera Velutini. This is the cycle of the Trump administration’s corruption: criminal cronyism, a donation, a pardon, and all of it completely out in the open. This open sale of justice occurred SEPARATELY from the Eric Adams case, where the Trump administration asked me to drop a case for political reasons. I refused, because my oath was to the Constitution and the rule of law, not the President. I have trained prosecutors worldwide on prosecuting corrupt public officials. And I can tell you that it doesn’t take long before corruption becomes a part of a nation’s culture. We are running out of time to stop the damage being done. | | **IMPORTANT NEWS FROM TEAM CRUZ ...** **[Sen. Cruz: Greenland Acquisition Key to US Interests:](https://trk.email.tedcruz.-eXbrpyinwHugZ431GOEw) "**Sen. Ted Cruz, R-Texas, backed President Donald Trump's push to acquire Greenland, calling the move "overwhelmingly" in the national interest. "When it comes to Greenland, I want to commend President Trump for being single-mindedly focused on America First, on U.S. economic interests, and U.S. national security interests," Cruz said, The Hill reported. "I believe it is overwhelmingly in America's national interest to acquire Greenland," he added in the interview on Fox News..." **[Cruz, Cornyn Bill Targets Repeat DUI Offenses by Illegal Aliens:](https://trk.email.tedcruz.) "**Both U.S. senators for the State of Texas say that more needs to be done when it comes to preventing those illegally present in the country from committing drunk driving offenses that result in serious injuries or death, and have filed joint legislation to address the issue. U.S. Sens. Ted Cruz (R-TX) and John Cornyn (R-TX) announced their bill, dubbed the Stop Illegal Aliens Drunk Driving Act, which would amend current law by making it an aggravated felony for any illegal alien to commit..." [**Ted Cruz 's 2026 Game Plan: Lose Weight, Skip The Drinks, Read The Bible Daily:**](https://trk.email.tedcruz._Wj_QMSNKmw) "Sen. Ted Cruz has three New Year's resolutions -- and he's making it his 2026 mission to stick to them. In an exclusive interview with Fox News Digital, Cruz, R-Texas, laid out his 2026 promises. "The first is one that a lot of people make in the New Year, which is hard to deliver on -- to lose some weight," Cruz said. "In particular, to drop 30 pounds this year." Cruz told Fox News Digital he weighed in at 236 pounds Jan. 1, 2026. "My goal by January of next year is to be 206," he said..." **[Ted Cruz Says 'Acquire Greenland': The 'Whole History' Of America Is One Of Acquiring More Lands:](https://trk.email.tedcruz.-16t_ETfxO1QDgpgg)** "Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Texas) said the United States should consider acquiring Greenland, arguing that the Arctic territory is strategically vital for national security, economic competitiveness and defense against global rivals. On Monday, Cruz made the remarks while sharing a clip of his Fox News interview on X, where he wrote, "Acquire Greenland." In the interview, the Texas Republican said it is "overwhelmingly in America's national interest to acquire Greenland..." **[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz._R5iDLP9oqFoqTMmAzZwHg)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.-lhJ-wEQ)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.-Nm9LhwlhDKpqRQ)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz._qBseW7g)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz._IApw)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[Send Ted Cruz Support Right Now>>](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz._78rYZf2wg)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.-TSSOnZidC9ghUA)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz._pakQ)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.-bQ)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz._0T9aQClDw)[](https://trk.email.tedcruz.)**

# [Why are Op-Eds The Only Campaign Attention in The Media Cycle?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=185739640&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1NzM5NjQwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjkzNjEzMTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MTk1MzMxMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XbcKl6ix32bhFi2B49HyohS1LNwKLKxwRsu3ExyEsmY)
### Connecticut has a Media Problem
| | [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81) and [Dr. Michael Goldstein](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)
---
| Jan 25

---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3a004fed-94a7-4860-8f23-bc7b3949502e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
There was a recent patch "article" about the upcoming Special Election for Fairfield's First Selectman and a claim that Tony Hwang is forcing a special election.
[Pledge your support](https://substack..nV-1bm34U0RRT_FQrsMaJeU1ECXr9x8LiGoHUQOHUT4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=185739640)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=185739640&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1NzM5NjQwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjkzNjEzMTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MTk1MzMxMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XbcKl6ix32bhFi2B49HyohS1LNwKLKxwRsu3ExyEsmY)
A vacancy in Fairfield leadership was created by the untimely passing of **Bill Gerber** , the First Selectman of Fairfield. Under Connecticut law, such a vacancy triggers a democratic process to select a successor. Allowing that process to proceed is the lawful and appropriate course in a representative democracy even if it required petitions. **Petitions are the only voice of the people against party rule and one-party rule.**
It is also important to clarify the role of Connecticut's Republican Town Committees (RTCs). While RTCs exist to endorse candidates, they are not--and should not be--the final arbiters of who serves in public office. Many voters are unfamiliar with how RTCs operate, and the will of a small committee must never supersede the voice of the broader electorate.
_**Committees advise; voters decide. If a candidate chooses to take their message directly to the people, articulate their positions, and earns support at the ballot box, that outcome should always carry greater weight.**_
**Connecticut has a Media Problem**
Unfortunately, this election for Fairfield's First Selectman has unfolded amid a troubling media environment. In an information age, voters should expect substantive, fact-based coverage of candidates, records, and policy differences. Instead, local media coverage has been sparse or visibly slanted, leaving the public primarily with competing and often biased op-eds from both sides. That does a disservice to voters and undermines informed decision-making.
Looking at the race objectively--and based on the limited debate exposure available through short television segments--it is clear that **Tony Hwang** is the more qualified candidate to lead Fairfield and bring fiscal discipline to town government.
Importantly, Senator Hwang has not been part of Fairfield's local budget-making process. His experience has been on the legislative side at the state level, providing an external perspective and the ability to challenge entrenched assumptions. There is also so much he can do in a Democrat Run supermajority in Hartford.
By contrast, **Christine** **Vitale** has been around and now serves Fairfield's governing structure, where there is direct accountability for the town's current fiscal trajectory, development patterns, and infrastructure strain. That record deserves serious scrutiny.
**Where Is the Tax Revenue?**
_**At the same time as this election there are property tax re-evaluations going on for Fairfield Residents while Vitale has been in office.**_
Vitale believes that residential property grows the tax base. She is mistaken. It has been 10 years since General Electric left Fairfield and housing revenue has not replaced it. The Alto Apartment Building that Vitale references generates roughly $770,000 less per year than the former GE commercial site.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/40803615-c117-4829-bd57-5b2b41cc1836?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Source Fairfield Republicans](https://substack.com/redirect/b90e2d4e-f5ef-4324-940b-4d824771dabc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**If voters are satisfied with the status quo --and with Fairfield continuing toward higher density, increased traffic, and growing pressure on municipal services and higher property taxes--then continuity may be acceptable. But if residents are looking for creative, out-of-the-box solutions to improve housing stock without overtaxing resources, while also curbing costs and reducing overall budget pressure, Tony Hwang represents a clear alternative.**
[Pledge your support](https://substack..nV-1bm34U0RRT_FQrsMaJeU1ECXr9x8LiGoHUQOHUT4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=185739640)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=185739640&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1NzM5NjQwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjkzNjEzMTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MTk1MzMxMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XbcKl6ix32bhFi2B49HyohS1LNwKLKxwRsu3ExyEsmY)
Much of the campaign rhetoric through op-eds or other opinion pieces focused on future elections, motives, and speculation is a distraction. It deflects from what truly matters: a direct, fact-based evaluation of the candidates, their records, and their governing philosophies.
Hysteria and polarization--locally and nationally--have distorted public discourse for years, accelerating during crises like COVID and long before.
**Fairfield, Connecticut, and the country would benefit from lowering the temperature and returning to sober, principled evaluation of leadership.**
That is how democracy is supposed to work. Media is supposed to be objective and provide a forum not just a place for anyone with a laptop and an email address to alarm people with no factual backup about their personal beliefs about a candidate they dislike.
_**Tony Hwang is not "forcing" a special election, nor is this situation the result of political gamesmanship because Amy LaRoche filed to run for State Senate and "challenge" him this November. This is America and if you want to run and put your name on a ballot and fight for a vote, you should be. There should be no entitlement in America. **_
Make your voices heard this February 3rd - and here is the information below on how to do it:
# **Fairfield Special Election on Tuesday, February 3, 2026**
**To elect a new First Selectperson**
**Special Election Day Details:**
* **Date:** Tuesday, February 3, 2026
* **Polling Hours:** 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m.
* **Polling Locations:** Your regular polling place (check your location here: [https://portaldir.ct.gov/sots/LookUp.aspx](https://substack.com/redirect/4aac43d8-5c96-42ce-9209-5da9d5b476f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))

**Absentee Ballots:**
* Absentee Ballot Applications are available now, [click here](https://substack.com/redirect/d8d00c8d-6555-493b-853f-68799b70b69c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to download an application.
* **You may also complete an absentee ballot in person** on weekdays starting on Monday, January 5, 2026 at the Town Clerk's Office, 611 Old Post Road, Fairfield, from 8:30 am to 4:30 pm. For more information, please call the Town Clerk at 203-256-3090.

**Early Voting:**
* Early voting will take place Thursday, January 29th, 2026, through Sunday, February 1st, 2026, between the hours of 10:00 a.m. and 6:00 p.m. at the Fairfield University Bookstore Downtown, 1499 Post Road, Fairfield.
* Additional parking is available in the rear of the building, please use the Sherman Street entrance.

| A guest post by| [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81?utm_campaign=guest_post_bio&utm_medium=email)Business Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: We have a choice

body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240634%2E444211%2EDCEEJk)
Neither of us had been running enough lately to feel good going into the race. We pushed each other to sign up, each in our own way. Amy raising the possibility and then saying oh, I don’t know if I can do a 10k right now. And me saying of course you can. I’m the one we should be worried about. Yeah she says but you’ve been running your whole life, this wouldn’t be that hard for you. And so in that way we boosted each other into running it.
We warmed up jogging from our home in El Paso’s Sunset Heights down to San Jacinto plaza, maybe half a mile. It was cold and I was worried about my banged up toes, battered on a couple of freezing early morning mountain trail runs over the last few weeks and just starting to return to normal. The rest of the year I’m ok running the mountain, but in the winter there’s something about being cold and running these rocky trails that does something to my toes. Painful. I read that if you keep your core warm your body is more willing to extend blood to your extremities like your feet, so I wore a vest on top of my T-shirt and overshirt.
We got to the starting area of the [**Run Internacional U.S. - Mexico 10k.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/67466?t=3&akid=240634%2E444211%2EDCEEJk) Most of the three thousand runners from El Paso and Juárez were already there, sprinting or jumping in place to warm up or talking with each other or looking for a place to pee before the race. We saw Mario and Steph who’ve been putting this run on for the last ten years with the Community Foundation and gave them a hug, took off that vest after all, and slipped into a spot 20 people deep from the start. There was maybe 5 or 6 minutes to go. I jumped around in place to keep warm, looked as far as I could see behind me and then to the right at the spectators in the park as the Mexican national anthem and then ours played out over the plaza. The other runners looking the way I felt. A little anxious, a little excited, wondering how we’d hold up over the course of the six miles in front of us.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260125-1.qW38ce&refcode=em20260125-240634&%3Bamounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=240634%2E444211%2EDCEEJk)
A voice on the PA counted down the final seconds and then we were off. You know what that looks like if you’ve ever run one of these. Shuffling, running, stopping because the person in front of you has stopped, walking, running again and then doing your best to both dodge those in the way and get out of the way of those dodging you. But no one went down or got hurt. A school of fish. Sometimes humans just figure it out.
Right away we were climbing. In the shadow of the triple-A Chihuahuas ballpark, the El Paso high school drum line was pounding and popping the code to get up this hill. I saw Amy in front of me and to the right but I couldn’t get close, too many people between us including some who were already walking. I moved to the other side of the street and zigzagged my way ahead.
From then on we were only barely connected. I’d look over my shoulder over the next mile and see Amy directly behind me and then a little later she’s to my right but further behind, and then finally I couldn’t see her. Usually she comes out strong and I’m the one catching up, so I figured she wasn’t feeling it today or was saving her strength.
Do I like running? I was asking myself. A little more than a mile in with five to go and my legs were heavy, my nose was running, my throat was dry. People were passing me on both sides. One person was wearing soccer cleats, another full-body flannel pajamas. They looked happy and at ease. I didn’t. The idea came into my head that maybe I wasn’t going to finish this race and should never run another one. No, I don’t like running at all I thought.
But... hearing that voice in my head that tells me to quit, and then not quitting, does something for me. I’ve passed this test of will and am now ready. For what? To finish the race. To run again, maybe. To keep going this time, and the next. Someone was holding a sign that said ¡Eres chingon! Each time my watch buzzed to tell me I’d completed another mile I felt a little stronger. I can do this. Maybe I do like running.
We ran past the Mexican consulate and then back through Segundo Barrio, the neighborhood of immigrants in a city of immigrants from which poets, revolutionaries and artists have come for as long as El Paso has existed. Segundo is where Lawrence Nixon practiced medicine and would later lead the fight to end racial segregation in voting; where Mariano Azuela—another physician turned activist—wrote Los de Abajo, the defining novel of the Mexican revolution; where Cimi Alvarado painted his murals and where Bert Williams and Nolan Richardson grew up, legends in sports and civil rights. We turned south on Stanton Street until it took us to the border, the toll collectors at the foot of the international bridge to Mexico cheering us on as we climbed the embankment to the top and then let our burning legs recover on the steep decline into Juárez.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260125-1.qW38ce&refcode=em20260125-240634&%3Bamounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=5&akid=240634%2E444211%2EDCEEJk)
_Passing the Mexican consulate in El Paso._
At the southern foot of the bridge a Juárez high school orchestra was playing Eye of the Tiger while the Mexican customs officers cheered and goaded the runners. Ya mero! one of them yelled. What’s he talking about? There’s four miles to go.
But at this point I’d found someone I could hang with, who was running my pace and seemed to be at the same level of fitness, mas o menos. I figured we’d help each other through. Was he American? Mexican? Neither of us could spare the energy to make conversation, there was no way to tell. But I was sure glad I found him, he kept me in the race.
We circled under the bridge and then headed due east for a mile or so, finally turning south and then west towards downtown Juárez. A guy just in front of me with big headphones over his head was singing a song in Spanish, in a big booming voice. How can he breathe and run this fast while singing this loud? I could barely stay with him. We passed a woman on the sidewalk holding a sign that said ¡No te conozco, pero eres mi héroe! We kept running towards the historic center of the city. Approaching the cathedral and the mission next to it that was founded in 1659, the strains of Dust in the Wind by Kansas belted out by a local artist hit my ears while my eyes were met by the first of many mimes.
They’d jump out at us from among the rest of the crowd every few minutes during that last mile and a half, black and white striped shirt, painted white face, yelling encouragement without making a sound, directing us to the big turn that would take us to Avenida Juárez, the home stretch.
Once I made that turn north, I knew I was going to be OK. Maybe I even looked happy. More mimes. Stray dogs. A couple of sharp looking high school kids dancing folklorico to a blaring boombox. People on both sides of the streets cheering, whistling, laughing, looking at their phones, taking a break from whatever a normal Saturday is to watch the race run by.
Kentucky Club on my left and then behind me. That’s where I took Amy on our first date. I remember Rodrigo was at the bar that night and we had margaritas with him before walking next door for dinner at Martinos. I turned to tell my new running partner that we were almost there but he was gone. So I focused on the next runner ahead, catching him after a few blocks. He recognized me and said Hey Beto! Let’s do this! And I said we’re almost there. He was breathing loudly, straining painful sounding breaths. I thought I probably sounded the same.
And then we came to the end of Avenida Juárez and began the final climb. Straight up the steep incline of the Paso del Norte bridge you go. The sister bridge of the one that we had crossed into Juárez. This one was bringing us back home.
As long as we could make it to the top.
But, of course, we did. That climb, which you think is going to kill you when you start it, and also when you’re in the middle of it, and just as you’re finishing it too, isn’t so bad after all. You emerge from it on top of the bridge, the finish line banner floating over the actual international boundary line. So your first footstep after the race is your first footstep back in the U.S.
I crossed the line, stopped the timer on my watch and not more than 10 seconds later Amy was next to me. She’d been gaining on me slowly ever since I thought I’d lost her. If the race had lasted another quarter mile she would have caught me.
Now on the U.S. side of the bridge, we caught our breath. Smiled, laughed about how hard that final climb was. The guy that I’d been running next to earlier in the race, the one who’d kept me in it when I needed it, came over and we shook hands, laughed and told each other we couldn’t have made it without the other. I still couldn’t tell which country he was from. Not that it matters. He was happy to be alive, to have finished the race, to have come through his doubt and pain. Just like me. We congratulated each other again, said see you out there.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260125-1.qW38ce&refcode=em20260125-240634&%3Bamounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=6&akid=240634%2E444211%2EDCEEJk)
_The team that put on the race: Sofia, Mario and Stephanie._
As I walked down the other side of the bridge—downtown El Paso ahead, Anapra Juárez to the left, the Franklin mountains to the right coming into full view—I ran into a Customs and Border Protection officer that I remember working with when I was in Congress. He’d help our office when we would request humanitarian assistance on behalf of a constituent, say someone in El Paso wanting to cross a family member to see their dad who lay dying at the county hospital; or a request to have a loved one come over for a funeral; or a Mexican national with a U.S. citizen child needing life-saving care. Almost always an emergency, often someone at the end of their life, a final wish. And he always tried to help, sometimes he could, sometimes it didn’t work out. But he understood the job. Vigilance against those who would harm us, those trying to break the law. Compassion towards those just doing their best at a trying time.
I wondered what he thought about all that has changed since then. [**Three people have died at the newly built ICE detention facility in east El Paso in just the last month and a half.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/67467?t=7&akid=240634%2E444211%2EDCEEJk) And the intense militarization of our border at the same time that there is no effort to open up legal, humanitarian paths for people to flee persecution, join family, or find work—it’s killing people. Hundreds will take their last breaths here, anonymous, uncared for, uncelebrated, unloved in their final hour. (176 recorded deaths of migrants in the El Paso Border Patrol sector in 2024, the last year for which DHS has published information. In 2018, by comparison, there were six.)
The Gestapo transformation of immigration enforcement. The murder of Renee Good. The idea that at home or abroad might make right, and no longer will we care about the law, or what is decent or what is good. It’s brutality. Barbarism. Is that who we are?
Or are we this? I looked out at these beautiful people gathered atop this bridge that joins the largest binational community on the planet. Each of us having just pushed ourselves, some to extremes, and yet no one getting ahead at the expense of anyone else. In fact, the whole spirit of this thing was that we were encouraging each other, cheering each other on, celebrating this amazing place we are lucky enough to live in. Though we’re told we must be divided from one another, we’ve instead come together.
By the end of the race, with everyone on the bridge, you could no longer see the international boundary line meant to mark the separation between us.
Was he seeing what I was seeing? We shook hands and then Amy and I kept walking down the bridge towards El Paso and home.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260125-1.qW38ce&refcode=em20260125-240634&%3Bamounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=8&akid=240634%2E444211%2EDCEEJk)
_The finish line looking north to El Paso._
Thank you,
Beto

name: GOP ALERTS | Rep. Burlison
subject: Bill and Hilary in a cold, dark jail cell?

subject: ICE excutions demand accountability.
body:
We've now learned that the man ICE executed in cold blood was named Alex Pretti. He was an ICU nurse, a husband, and a pillar of his community. He lost his life because he was peacefully participating in protests against an out of control fascist regime.
His blood is on the hands of every elected official who supports unchecked ICE funding and defends the violence being perpetrated by the Trump regime.
Remember our saying: OUTRAGE. Then, action.
We're raising funds for candidates who will vote to ABOLISH ICE once and for all. If you support this mission and would like to take action, join us by making a donation. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/outrage-then-action?refcode=012526_1]
🚨🚨🚨🚨🚨🚨
BREAKING OUT OF MINNEAPOLIS
ICE tackles, publicly executes protestor in broad daylight
Just over an hour ago, ICE agents in Minneapolis tackled a protestor on a sidewalk and shot him point blank in the head. He died at the scene.
The horror you felt reading that sentence is justified and correct. We feel the same. The question now is - what do we do next?
We have a saying around here:
OUTRAGE. Then, action.
We're doubling down on raising funds for candidates who are committed to:
1: Holding EVERY Republican who allowed ICE to run rampant in our streets accountable, and
2: Voting to ABOLISH ICE once and for all
If you want someplace to channel your outrage - join us now by chipping in to help REMOVE as many ICE supporters from office as possible. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/outrage-then-action?refcode=012526_1]

body:
Trump’s veto fits a broader pattern |
---

| | _**The New York Times: “A Trump Veto Leaves Republicans in Colorado Parched and Bewildered”**_ At first glance, this might not look like a story about a failed attempt to overthrow an election. But with Donald Trump, many roads lead back to his easily bruised ego and his refusal to accept the fact that he lost in 2020. Recently, he vetoed a bipartisan bill that Congress had passed unanimously to help ensure people in rural Colorado had access to clean drinking water. And his action seems to fit into a broader pressure campaign against the state — moving the Space Force headquarters out of Colorado, trying to keep transportation and child care funding away from the state, and shutting down a critical scientific research center in Boulder. Why Colorado? Well, he’s also been calling for the release of his MAGA ally Tina Peters. She was a county clerk in Colorado and was convicted and sentenced to prison for sharing unauthorized access to the county election system when she was trying to back up unfounded conspiracy theories about so-called fraud in the 2020 election. Now Trump seems willing to punish the people of Colorado until she’s free to try to overthrow another election. **These are the actions of a petty, vindictive bully who refuses to accept defeat at the ballot box. Who won’t rest until he’s consolidated control. ** [**But we are fighting back against his MAGA movement’s attacks on democracy. name: Bank Account alert, Jacob Davis | FIU
subject: Do THESE 4 things to your bank account now… ∕ Sunday, January 25, 2026
body:

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

EMILYs List women in the U.S. House and Senate are the ones leading the fight to combat RFK Jr.'s dangerous disinformation campaign. clean_body: His playbook? Manufacture doubt, undermine trust, and restrict access. | = Hi Peter, RFK Jr. is continuing to weaponize his position as head of the Department of Health and Human Services to target mifepristone, an abortion medication with a 25-year safety record that's been used by more than seven million people. His playbook? Manufacture doubt, undermine trust, and restrict access. = | = Last fall, RFK Jr. and the FDA's leadership moved to initiate a new FDA review of mifepristone, reopening questions that anti-choice extremists are using to push for medically unnecessary restrictions. We've seen this before, Peter. This is the exact same tactic he used with Tylenol — spreading baseless fears to pregnant women about autism risks, despite decades of evidence proving the safety of acetaminophen. Medical experts shut him down then, and we'll shut him down now. EMILYs List women in the U.S. House and Senate are the ones leading the fight to combat RFK Jr.'s dangerous disinformation campaign. This cycle, we can elect even more Democratic pro-choice women who will be the stopgap between the Trump administration's anti-science, anti-choice agenda and the vast majority of Americans, who are pro-choice. **=** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Talk soon, Amanda Sherman Baity (she/her/hers) Chief Communications Officer, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. Fellow Patriot,

I’m going to be brutally honest with you.

Arizona is _ground zero._

If Democrats get even a little bit of power back in 2026, they’re going to turn our state into a political punching bag and a testing ground for their next wave of insanity. And that starts with flipping seats like this one. They don’t hate me because of my “tone” or because I’m “too conservative.” **They hate me because I’ve done the job they refuse to do:**

I fought cartels.
I chased fentanyl traffickers.
I watched families get ripped apart because politicians in D.C. turned the border into a joke.

[**And now I’m running for Congress because I’m sick of watching career politicians talk tough on TV while Americans pay the price.**](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a03b72662fa7444194a62caab619b058&_e=OqK8XnvPNbg8iWdo5W8CPDZrnKJjNxc3ghPkQIqe3SggRwvellBG1D7AGTzL0ldLSiRjzXlgZI2mj4F83DtLlFYjs4_eYYlB6FUW6naEsM5DzC8kk6i4IR4fT2X7ttmKxexBvRwH_pdh_rRDJxyZ67fx3R-AyWWH5PSVZDwybMK_MznvVM-4QAIE3IVw4idetaGQ4sHVWlY7TVyreLAMDp2SsNarwXCDi63OOz8CgpcVRX7ABhVKmnpgrp8M-yT2InBQvScwWmcscBTRunFRGiOc9bDIKrSGW5O1Cb4nCJiEkIkx7EBx3A0F_qX3S59RRkdx0eFVsrKDU5Py2WlnjHgo0zRciaJZWV5jAr6ibwjXq5wdtekWkMADOGbTJFQQtZNHLMBZ12xifl88GXj5T_0SmX6f7QqMvjmkMDE6IfxZntpgKaglRXhio1ZXRP8VYiOLIxzgdJ58sXCdpoCufdkhF2FMcMI4egkhB0k9ZKKJdGlVvrurM3UQUSImAyp-qq8Dp_uB5IWDe5p4cQ8HKR7Iu9VFYIS_zrurWrW7MfcJlY4QgWSklhI4gMrED4Im-sw5-mokVeJI8NA04obCrJ81u5ENJS4wA1pILct5CrR2su3dvbKR4ngvBtb9Bh5S6tvrb63GnHcm6NAL7pSECsNeKu2IkMDcnyiCnp8eWLA%3D)

**Here’s what the Democrats want:**

They want open-border chaos to keep going.
They want the fentanyl pipeline wide open.
They want Americans to stay overwhelmed, unsafe, and ignored.

And if they flip the House? They will come after EVERY SINGLE Republican who dared challenge their socialist policies.

**They’ll use the gavel to impeach President Trump, block the America First agenda, and spend two years doing investigations and political theater while our border continues to collapse.**

[**That’s why I’m asking: If you can chip in right now — even $10 or $20 — please do it.**](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a03b72662fa7444194a62caab619b058&_e=OqK8XnvPNbg8iWdo5W8CPDZrnKJjNxc3ghPkQIqe3SggRwvellBG1D7AGTzL0ldLSiRjzXlgZI2mj4F83DtLlFYjs4_eYYlB6FUW6naEsM5DzC8kk6i4IR4fT2X7ttmKxexBvRwH_pdh_rRDJxyZ67fx3R-AyWWH5PSVZDwybMK_MznvVM-4QAIE3IVw4idetaGQ4sHVWlY7TVyreLAMDp2SsNarwXCDi63OOz8CgpeH52wn5CTRKRedWpXnkfp-fjkeR6MsOzPc-oaaoMHlLMxsyPhKbo56iiNVM-xAoBNbr-JfcVNvSmRROPqAN8TZXclvh4GvgDYh3zYpDPl3kA5DU6zsS66yNeODCqDzcVpXB-EKD82tDEiiECQfRGJC39FF03oZut-uWcsMPHwpr8MbsiZRxi2V3fihpv0dCx-PGkgoYXIoLpJuqq-bEsST3IpgmISxouylbPEqCT4dTpgZ6IP2tm1Kjoonoc0kLpDMLFsJ1kVYU07v-kPzbSeXxhjN1NxmV9X8H-QE5CdCMjrr1d17GEpjNJYgglgojLrQYKFndLCdImiQ8WrSYunRYRUDtIO0lsCLoxAqPxMeSLO8b5Yy8t5fgyU9tozweL6zYY7YLGbVrUvjcxvmwCxhX3PuAlUggO41aLZa7eSdZD-nCjnbu33pjXc3eLa-yGY%3D)

Because the radical left is going to dump millions into Arizona—they already are. And I’m not backing down. I’m running to secure the border, crush the cartels, and finally put America First.

clean_body: | Fellow Patriot, I’m going to be brutally honest with you. Arizona is ground zero. If Democrats get even a little bit of power back in 2026, they’re going to turn our state into a political punching bag and a testing ground for their next wave of insanity. And that starts with flipping seats like this one. They don’t hate me because of my “tone” or because I’m “too conservative.” They hate me because I’ve done the job they refuse to do: I fought cartels. I chased fentanyl traffickers. I watched families get ripped apart because politicians in D.C. turned the border into a joke. Here’s what the Democrats want: They want open-border chaos to keep going. They want the fentanyl pipeline wide open. They want Americans to stay overwhelmed, unsafe, and ignored. And if they flip the House? They will come after EVERY SINGLE Republican who dared challenge their socialist policies. They’ll use the gavel to impeach President Trump, block the America First agenda, and spend two years doing investigations and political theater while our border continues to collapse. Because the radical left is going to dump millions into Arizona—they already are. And I’m not backing down. I’m running to secure the border, crush the cartels, and finally put America First. But I need backup. — Mark Lamb Join Mark Lamb on social media! | | PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go: .

**But our fight isn’t over.** Now, our extreme opponent and her billionaire backers are on high alert.
While we’re building a people-powered campaign, our opponent is doubling down on billionaires to save her. They see us as a threat, and they’re preparing to drown this race in outside money.

### Donald Trump's Totalitarian Storm Troopers Can No Longer Be Tolerated
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6e2ef87b-a257-4d75-a6f1-a6a7d7ca11e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Cliff 's Note:** _After my rant a handful of Senate Democrats planning to weakly join 7 House Democrats in screwing the rest of us --funding Trump's low-rent SS--have come around. Supporting **good** Democrats, winning, and enacting our values requires that we challenge the sellouts, and weak leadership when it threatens us. I will keep doing it._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6e2ef87b-a257-4d75-a6f1-a6a7d7ca11e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I'm done pretending this is complicated.
**Alex Pretti was almost certainly murdered by federal agents in Minneapolis** , and everything that followed--the excuses, the delays, the finger-pointing--was designed to make all of us tired, confused, and numb. That's how dictators operate now.
The flaccid toy soldiers of convicted felon Donald Trump flood the zone with enough horseshit to hamper accountability for assassins, while another American lies dead in our streets courtesy of the Schutzstaffel. So let's slow this down and cut through the crap. Here are **five things you need to know** about the Alex Pretti murder--and why it should terrify and enrage anyone who wants to live in a functioning democracy.
1. **This Was a Public Execution, Not an "Incident"**
Let's stop soft-pedaling the language. A man was tackled in the street by multiple federal agents and **shot five times on camera**. Another woman was **shot in the face**. A 5-year old child was **kidnapped while his mom begged for him to be returned to her at home**. That's not law enforcement error, it's lethal force and totalitarianism with with total confidence there'll be no consequences. If any civilian did this, _**we 'd already be in prison**_.
2. **ICE Is Operating as a Masked, Unaccountable Paramilitary**
These agents don't identify themselves. They wear masks. They ignore basic due process. [They violate state law and the Constitution in plain sight](https://substack.com/redirect/6e01aef1-c2df-4468-ad78-74a303b78d04?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). This is exactly what secret police look like. Pretending otherwise doesn't make it less true. You can believe in closely monitoring your borders, _**this is unrelated criminal behavior**_.
3. **Congress Is Complicit: Including Some Democrats**
**Congress funds this.** Look, we know virtually every Republican is a fascist-enabling MAGA ghoul for a [moronic Mad King-let out of his mind](https://substack.com/redirect/091bf5ff-a603-4688-8242-6922e85f6ff6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Still, seven House Democrats voted to fund DHS and ICE _after_ Renee Good was shot in the face. After viral video of an ICE agent with an SS tattoo threatening civilians.
Funding is permission. Votes are messages about what is good and acceptable. Those seven Democrats attacked America, our democracy, and the Democratic Party, giving a thumbs up to ICE. They even provided the one-vote margin Republicans needed to pass a [despicable, anti-constitutional](https://substack.com/redirect/2841b73d-2eed-4bb4-b7fb-3d896f22d363?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) bill.
4. **Democratic Leadership Failed to Stop It**
Either House Democratic leadership approved the vote, or they were too weak to stop the seven chuds. Those are the only options. **A functioning caucus does not allow seven members to enable state violence**. If you can't hold your own people accountable, you're not leading. If Hakeem Jeffries won't lead, he must step aside.
5. **Accountability Still Exists, If Anyone Has the Courage to Use It**
Governors, attorneys general, and district attorneys have the authority to act. Federal agents are not above state law. Arrest them. Prosecute them. Call the National Guard if necessary and let Trump fight it in court. And the rest of us? Show up. Protest. Primary the complicit. Be nonviolent, _**but don 't let this stand.**_
* **Cliff 's Note:** _Friends, I was more profane than usual in my furious rant. Yet, I couldn 't come close to using the profanity of masked, armed, unidentified goons pushing over a 37-yr old registered nurse as he tried to help a woman in distress, beating him, and shooting him 10 times, ripping his life from his body._
* * *

2. Yesterday I opened [@EpochTimes ](https://substack.com/redirect/a897e3fd-b6b2-4570-bfa4-8e9d882ed444?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and froze. They published my article--in print. Grateful. Humbled. Ready to keep going.
By Kathy Barnette
3. If the first week of 2026 is any indication of what lies ahead, we are in for a wild and bumpy ride - one that may prove to be among the most consequential years of our lives.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fb77aec7-ed93-4770-856e-2662e1a5eb47?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
4. If you have health insurance, you know it's very expensive. One of the major drivers of health insurance costs is the complex "paperwork," or administrative burden of our system.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5742bd84-e044-4ceb-b5dc-36271107e172?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

5. Kari Lake To Congress: Please Don't Give Us the Money
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e983bba8-8871-4e92-924b-d8210e8c0383?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In this interview, Kari Lake said something I've never heard a bureaucrat say out loud: "Please don't give us the money." She's talking about shrinking government, not growing it -- and it's a direct challenge to the "more funding fixes everything" mindset.
6. What's really breaking MAGA apart -- and is the chaos organic or engineered?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4f83868d-0df8-4482-a559-ecf4a4035170?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

clean_body: I need you to fill out our Voter Verification Survey by Midnight. The Radical Left is pulling out all the stops to undermine our democracy and silence conservative voices. I know we can stop them. I know we can take back control of our country and restore the values that made America great in the first place. That's why I'm counting on you to take action today. This is about sending a powerful message to the Democrats that we won't be intimidated, we won't be silenced, and we won't be defeated. before it closes this evening. Will you do that for me? Will you do that for our country? Thank you in advance. Sincerely, Jordan| | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
