name: Jon Ossoff for Georgia
subject: Not a DIME of corporate PAC money
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

During Jon’s last Senate campaign, hundreds of thousands of people donated to help flip this seat and we didn’t take a DIME of corporate PAC money. It’s something we’re _incredibly_ proud of.
But it’s a different race this time around, and Jon is counting on our help. And the harsh truth is that what we've raised so far simply isn’t enough. In order to win, we’re going to have to go _even bigger_ and we have to do it _right away._
MAGA has already spent millions on this race, and with so much on the line, you can expect lots of Super PAC money and Trump visits to defeat us.
You can expect wild attacks from our MAGA extremist opponents and from Trump and his cronies online. And then, of course, the things we can’t predict that we’ll have to be ready for...
**So we’re humbly asking for your help:**
**[Please, make a $10 contribution to help Jon defend this U.S. Senate seat in Georgia. We wouldn’t ask if your support didn’t make a real difference, Peter.](https://act.electjon.com/go/2122?c0=ossoff-em-jan26.SyHeIP&refcode=em260125-1-f&t=5&akid=1495%2E5691347%2ELPezDP)**
Thank you, sincerely, for everything you’re doing to help reelect Jon and keep MAGA out of the Senate.
Team Ossoff

Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/signup/fewer-emails-signup/?t=6&akid=1495%2E5691347%2ELPezDP) to sign up to receive fewer emails, or click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=1495%2E5691347%2ELPezDP) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

**" An arctic storm is expected to blanket the state. Here's what Texans can expect."** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/0f3b978d-d1a8-4876-8466-9238d6ccdb63?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Colleen DeGuzman** - Texans are bracing for a harsh winter storm that's expected to plunge the entire state into subfreezing temperatures starting Friday, bringing an arctic mix of snow, sleet and freezing rain.

Frigid air is blowing in from northern Canada, and according to National Weather Service Meteorologist **Kurt Van Speybroeck** , it's expected to reach the very southern tip of Texas by Sunday morning.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/cfe55559-2d55-4641-83dc-e316289fd147?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70//yr](https://substack.com/redirect/cfe55559-2d55-4641-83dc-e316289fd147?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Texans from Wichita Falls west to Lubbock and Amarillo should be preparing for snowy weather late Friday. The cold air and precipitation is expected to move south Saturday, and by Sunday morning, residents in Brownsville should expect temperatures to dip below freezing. Every part of the state, from El Paso to the Houston region, will experience a hard freeze during the storm, Speybroeck said.

"By Sunday, the whole state will definitely be experiencing well-below normal temperatures and dangerous wind chills," Speybroeck said.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/f5390e1d-48a1-44c6-a255-35658e4561c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Federal appeals court to rehear challenge against Texas' immigration law"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/377f46bb-a13b-4573-9bbc-922c14643ab3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Alejandro Serrano** - Three years ago, Texas lawmakers passed a landmark law that would let state police arrest people suspected of having entered the country illegally by crossing the border.

Such authority has long been the sole responsibility of the federal government, but the state's Republican leaders said Texas, which shares about 1,250 miles of border with Mexico, had a constitutional right to protect its sovereignty as the number of illegal border crossings hit record highs under the Biden administration.

But when a federal appeals court reheard oral arguments Thursday in a legal challenge seeking to stop Texas' law from going into effect, the circumstances outside the courtroom were much different.

The number of people crossing the border has dwindled to a trickle during President **Donald Trump** 's first year back in the White House, setting record lows in a sharp reversal from the influx that inspired Texas' law, known as Senate Bill 4. The new reality on the ground could complicate Texas' legal argument that it has a constitutional right to defend itself against an invasion, like the one state lawmakers claimed was afoot when they enacted the law.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/011c28b3-dfe0-4855-a4a7-4dcc350a3e36?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Federal court focuses on informant in appeal of Texas death row inmate's conviction"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/14b63c44-a3bc-4124-9c9f-9d2cd76bcd4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Ayden Runnels** - The 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals on Wednesday heard oral arguments once again on the conviction of Texas death row inmate **Brittany Holberg** , which was previously overturned by justices in March.

All 17 of the court's judges listened to arguments and questioned attorneys for both Holberg and the state as they weighed the appeal. Much of the discussion and questions from the judges revolved around **Vickie Kirkpatrick** , who was Holberg's cellmate before her trial.

Holberg was convicted of capital murder in 1998 for killing and robbing 80-year-old **A.B. Towery** , whom she stabbed 58 times and shoved an 11-inch piece of lamp post down his throat while he was still alive. Holberg is one of seven women on death row.

Kirkpatrick became the key witness during trial when she testified that Holberg told her she went to Towery's home to rob him, enjoyed killing Towery and would do so again for drugs or money. Prosecutors did not disclose at trial, however, that Kirkpatrick had been working as a paid informant for the City of Amarillo on several other cases.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/c77bb419-ae08-4e09-a926-78a12cc54073?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)
* * *
* **LOCAL GOVERNMENT**

**" Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar, brother of Rep. Henry Cuellar, indicted on federal fraud charges"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/5b59ef9c-b5fa-4fb8-9efe-2e209e569690?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Eleanor Klibanoff** - **Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar** appeared before a federal judge Thursday for allegedly using public funds to run a fraudulent for-profit disinfecting business during the COVID-19 pandemic. He has pleaded not guilty and was released on bond.

The South Texas sheriff was indicted alongside his assistant chief in November. The charges, unsealed Thursday, accuse them of opening a private business, Disinfect Pro Master, in April 2020 and entering into agreements with local businesses and restaurants "despite having no employees or supplies of their own."

The company secured a $500,000 contract to clean schools in Laredo, which they fulfilled using department staff and resources, U.S. Attorney **Nicholas Ganjei** of the Southern District of Texas said in a statement.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/94d40eb7-64e6-4e2a-8a44-0560980e5bb2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Texas counties, auto industry push back on proposed ID restrictions on car registration tied to immigration"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/2be2a45f-4c49-41fd-ac15-db1b85b91107?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Alex Nguyen** - The Texas Department of Motor Vehicles is facing pushback to its proposed rule changes adding stricter photo identification requirements for vehicle registrations, which would prevent undocumented residents from legally owning vehicles.

The DMV on Wednesday heard public testimonies opposing the plan from around 35 people representing small businesses, industry groups, county tax assessor offices and advocacy organizations from across the state. The proposed changes would not just create more barriers for a broad swath of people to live and work in a car-dependent state, but also harm Texas businesses and road safety, they said.

"This rule change also creates a contradictory outcome for the state of Texas," said **Tyler Simmons** , a Texas Independent Automobile Dealers Association's board member.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/4926195e-772c-45b9-8e33-48e3e993c254?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" North Texas school district cancels Islamic Games for alleged ties to group governor calls terroristic"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/c9467e4a-b67e-4df7-91c9-3b67268eeb37?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Fort Worth Report** - Grapevine-Colleyville ISD officials "severed negotiations" with an Islamic group seeking to host an all-ages sporting tournament at a local high school, a district spokesperson confirmed to the Fort Worth Report on Tuesday.

The decision came after school administrators were "made aware" that one of the event's sponsors, the Council on American-Islamic Relations, or CAIR, was designated a terrorist organization by Gov. Greg Abbott last year, GCISD spokesperson **Nicole Lyons** said in an email.

But organizers of the Islamic Games said CAIR "has never been, and is not presently, a sponsor, partner, affiliate or supporter" of the sports festival.

"We are deeply concerned that a sporting event for children is being targeted," **Salaudeen Nausrudeen** , president of Islamic Games, said in an email to the Report. "It is our hope that better minds prevail."

Islamic Games officials requested to use the Colleyville Heritage High School to host its Dallas chapter's gaming event in May, Lyons said.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/61d4925b-960f-41a1-8981-1345cdf0f83e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)
* * *
* **2026**

**" Jasmine Crockett, James Talarico show different fighting styles in first debate of Senate Democratic primary" **[Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/578e488b-8f0b-436b-b722-f878dfb1d2c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Kayla Guo** - U.S. Rep. **Jasmine Crockett** and state Rep.**James Talarico b** oth pitched themselves as fighters Saturday during their first, and perhaps only, debate in the Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, with Crockett framing herself as a "street fighter" and Talarico leaning into his record battling GOP proposals in the Texas House.

The two Democrats debated for an hour at the Texas AFL-CIO's political convention in Georgetown ahead of the March 3 election. Early voting begins Feb. 17, just over three weeks out from Saturday.

Crockett, D-Dallas, and Talarico, D-Austin, are competing for their party's nomination to take on the eventual Republican nominee in November, when Democrats hope that backlash to the Trump administration -- and a GOP nominee damaged from a bruising primary battle -- will help lift the party to its first statewide victory in over three decades. U.S. Sen. **John Cornyn** , the Republican incumbent, is fighting to defend his seat in a bitter primary against Attorney General **Ken Paxton** and U.S. Rep. **Wesley Hunt** of Houston.

Crockett has pitched her candidacy, defined by her reputation as a political brawler, as the one best positioned to expand the electorate, with a focus on juicing Democratic turnout and motivating low-propensity voters to show up at the polls. Talarico, meanwhile, has emphasized his Christian faith and a top-versus-bottom brand of economic populism, betting those tenets can win over disaffected Republican and independent voters while also engaging the Democratic base.

During the debate, Crockett emphasized her ability to tap into the "rawness of this moment" by taking the fight to Republicans outside Capitol Hill, such as in Minneapolis during the Trump administration's ongoing immigration crackdown. She said this made her best positioned to motivate turnout and win in November.

"I am not just sitting in D.C.," she said. "I am getting out into the streets. And right now, to be perfectly honest, if we're going to win and keep our country, we're going to need somebody who can file bills but also knows how to be a street fighter."

Talarico, meanwhile, highlighted his experience as a middle school teacher and his record fighting "tooth and nail" in the Texas House, where he frequently went viral over clips of his exchanges with Republicans on proposals including private school vouchers and a mandate to display the Ten Commandments in public school classrooms.

"The best indicator of future behavior is past behavior," he said. "I have gone toe to toe with the billionaires who are trying to dismantle public education in this state."

The two candidates aligned on most policy issues that came up Saturday, including raising taxes on billionaires, repealing President **Donald Trump 's** tariffs, banning the sale of offensive weapons to Israel and using congressional authority to check the Trump administration's foreign policy in Venezuela, Greenland and Iran.

(_[Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/578e488b-8f0b-436b-b722-f878dfb1d2c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Congressman Wesley Hunt draws scrutiny for missed votes amid campaign for U.S. Senate"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/07f50c14-8abe-4f18-b3f5-308d5e2b578b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Olivia Borgula** - U.S. Rep. **Wesley Hunt** drew renewed criticism for his attendance record on Thursday after narrowly making it to Washington to clinch the GOP's defeat of a resolution that would have blocked further military action in Venezuela.

It marked only the fourth time Hunt has cast a vote on the House floor this year, out of 48 votes held in the lower chamber since lawmakers resumed business earlier this month. The absences come as the Houston Republican is waging a primary challenge against U.S. Sen **John Cornyn** , who has seized on Hunt's spotty voting record with a series of jabs on social media.

A Hunt spokesperson defended the absences, saying that no "bills, resolutions, or GOP priorities have been delayed or stopped because of Rep. Hunt's priority of barnstorming Texas to retire John Cornyn." On Friday, responding to what he described as "anyone clutching pearls about missed votes," Hunt added on social media that he has been "crisscrossing Texas nonstop" on the campaign trail.

"Anytime you run for higher office, you miss votes. Period," Hunt posted.

Speaker **Mike Johnson** , R-Louisiana, held Thursday's vote open for more than 20 minutes while the chamber awaited Hunt's arrival, prompting shouts from Democrats on the House floor urging Johnson to close the vote.

Hunt's 92% rate of missed votes this year has elevated his career mark to 20%, or missing one in five votes since he took office in 2023. Thursday's vote was Hunt's first since Jan. 7.

The median percentage of missed votes among current representatives is 2%, according to [GovTrack](https://substack.com/redirect/ad4d8bc9-5ea9-4e7a-aeac-b123890d3f73?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a nonprofit website that monitors congressional voting records. Rep.**Chip Roy** , an Austin Republican who is also running for statewide office in a four-way primary for attorney general, has not missed any votes this month. U.S. Rep. **Jasmine Crockett** , D-Dallas, has missed two as she runs opposite Hunt in the Democratic Senate primary.

Cornyn, who has missed one vote this year, has repeatedly bashed Hunt on social media over his attendance, while his campaign has launched a website, [whereswesleyhunt.com](https://substack.com/redirect/7374ddaf-e4d3-4ec1-8951-16156d7d0243?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), to highlight the issue.

"Wesley Hunt is refusing to fight for Trump's agenda by putting his selfish political ambitions before his responsibilities," Cornyn campaign spokesperson **Matt Mackowiak** said in a statement. "He needs to do his job."

In the primary, Hunt also faces Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, who has remained quiet on the issue.

([Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/07f50c14-8abe-4f18-b3f5-308d5e2b578b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))

**" James Talarico unveils Spanish-language TV ad, signaling push for Latino support in Texas Senate bid"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/3f5f12d7-a541-4ab9-907d-ef63f89c21d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Gabby Birenbaum** - **James Talarico** on Wednesday announced a new Spanish-language ad buy, set to air on Univision in nearly a dozen of the state's largest media markets, in what amounts to his first significant push to shore up support from Latino voters as he battles U.S. Rep. **Jasmine Crockett** for the Democratic nomination in Texas' U.S. Senate race.

The Spanish-language ad, shared first with The Texas Tribune, focuses on Talarico's defense of public education as a teacher and state legislator. It will air twice during Thursday's international friendly soccer match between Mexico and Panama in 11 media markets across the state. Talarico's campaign declined to disclose how much money was behind the ad buy.

Titled "¡Vale La Pena!", or "Worth It!", the ad describes Talarico's work in the state House to raise teacher pay and combat private school vouchers. (_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/ec83e454-6418-4482-bef3-7deabb9eedc6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" New red-leaning congressional seat in North Texas draws wide-open GOP primary field"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/3099ccd3-7408-4511-b844-430762415d4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Olivia Borgula** - Last year's redistricting has drawn a flurry of candidates campaigning for Texas' 32nd Congressional District, a seat that will almost certainly go to a Republican under new boundaries that stretch from Dallas into red-leaning East Texas.

Of the nine Republicans vying for Rep. **Julie Johnson** 's seat in the crowded March primary, notable names include **Ryan Binkley** , a businessman and pastor who self-funded a long-shot presidential bid in 2024; **Jace Yarbrough** , an attorney, conservative activist and former state Senate candidate; **Darrell Day** , the district's Republican nominee last cycle; and **Paul Bondar** , who ran for Oklahoma's 4th District in 2024.

Johnson, a freshman Democrat from Farmers Branch, was drawn out of her seat last summer by Republicans in the Texas Legislature, who crafted a new congressional map designed to net up to five seats for the GOP in the 2026 midterms. With her once solidly blue district now virtually out of reach for Democrats, Johnson is running for a nearby seat that contains about a third of her current constituents.

The redrawn 32nd District stretches from the Dallas suburbs into rural East Texas.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/2ec0993f-9e06-4159-b164-d98c632ef11d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Under new postal service guidance, Texans shouldn't wait to send voter registrations or mail-in ballots"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/e3f9684f-2c13-4dfa-9522-f0de4ecc999a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Natalia Contreras** - Texans seeking to register to vote or cast a ballot by mail may not want to wait until the last minute, thanks to new guidance from the U.S. Postal Service.

The USPS last month advised that it may not postmark a piece of mail on the same day that it takes possession of it. Postmarks are applied once mail reaches a processing facility, it said, which may not be the same day it's dropped in a mailbox, for example.

The new policy means that even if a voter drops their mail ballot in a box by Election Day, it could be rejected if it's not postmarked on that day. A voter registration application also could miss being postmarked by the Feb. 2 deadline.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/09c908cc-267d-4229-b47e-accf4794e2d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

Thank you for reading as a FREE subscriber. » Become a PAID subscriber for $7/mo or $70/yr . Hope you are staying warm! A WORD FROM SENATOR PHIL GRAMM ABOUT MUST READ TEXAS " If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | | ---|---|--- Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . TOP NEWS " An arctic storm is expected to blanket the state. Here's what Texans can expect." Colleen DeGuzman - Texans are bracing for a harsh winter storm that's expected to plunge the entire state into subfreezing temperatures starting Friday, bringing an arctic mix of snow, sleet and freezing rain. Frigid air is blowing in from northern Canada, and according to National Weather Service Meteorologist Kurt Van Speybroeck , it's expected to reach the very southern tip of Texas by Sunday morning. Texans from Wichita Falls west to Lubbock and Amarillo should be preparing for snowy weather late Friday. The cold air and precipitation is expected to move south Saturday, and by Sunday morning, residents in Brownsville should expect temperatures to dip below freezing. Every part of the state, from El Paso to the Houston region, will experience a hard freeze during the storm, Speybroeck said. "By Sunday, the whole state will definitely be experiencing well-below normal temperatures and dangerous wind chills," Speybroeck said. () " Federal appeals court to rehear challenge against Texas' immigration law" Alejandro Serrano - Three years ago, Texas lawmakers passed a landmark law that would let state police arrest people suspected of having entered the country illegally by crossing the border. Such authority has long been the sole responsibility of the federal government, but the state's Republican leaders said Texas, which shares about 1,250 miles of border with Mexico, had a constitutional right to protect its sovereignty as the number of illegal border crossings hit record highs under the Biden administration. But when a federal appeals court reheard oral arguments Thursday in a legal challenge seeking to stop Texas' law from going into effect, the circumstances outside the courtroom were much different. The number of people crossing the border has dwindled to a trickle during President Donald Trump 's first year back in the White House, setting record lows in a sharp reversal from the influx that inspired Texas' law, known as Senate Bill 4. The new reality on the ground could complicate Texas' legal argument that it has a constitutional right to defend itself against an invasion, like the one state lawmakers claimed was afoot when they enacted the law. () " Federal court focuses on informant in appeal of Texas death row inmate's conviction" Ayden Runnels - The 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals on Wednesday heard oral arguments once again on the conviction of Texas death row inmate Brittany Holberg , which was previously overturned by justices in March. All 17 of the court's judges listened to arguments and questioned attorneys for both Holberg and the state as they weighed the appeal. Much of the discussion and questions from the judges revolved around Vickie Kirkpatrick , who was Holberg's cellmate before her trial. Holberg was convicted of capital murder in 1998 for killing and robbing 80-year-old A.B. Towery , whom she stabbed 58 times and shoved an 11-inch piece of lamp post down his throat while he was still alive. Holberg is one of seven women on death row. Kirkpatrick became the key witness during trial when she testified that Holberg told her she went to Towery's home to rob him, enjoyed killing Towery and would do so again for drugs or money. Prosecutors did not disclose at trial, however, that Kirkpatrick had been working as a paid informant for the City of Amarillo on several other cases. () LOCAL GOVERNMENT " Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar, brother of Rep. Henry Cuellar, indicted on federal fraud charges" Eleanor Klibanoff - Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar appeared before a federal judge Thursday for allegedly using public funds to run a fraudulent for-profit disinfecting business during the COVID-19 pandemic. He has pleaded not guilty and was released on bond. The South Texas sheriff was indicted alongside his assistant chief in November. The charges, unsealed Thursday, accuse them of opening a private business, Disinfect Pro Master, in April 2020 and entering into agreements with local businesses and restaurants "despite having no employees or supplies of their own." The company secured a $500,000 contract to clean schools in Laredo, which they fulfilled using department staff and resources, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei of the Southern District of Texas said in a statement. () " Texas counties, auto industry push back on proposed ID restrictions on car registration tied to immigration" Alex Nguyen - The Texas Department of Motor Vehicles is facing pushback to its proposed rule changes adding stricter photo identification requirements for vehicle registrations, which would prevent undocumented residents from legally owning vehicles. The DMV on Wednesday heard public testimonies opposing the plan from around 35 people representing small businesses, industry groups, county tax assessor offices and advocacy organizations from across the state. The proposed changes would not just create more barriers for a broad swath of people to live and work in a car-dependent state, but also harm Texas businesses and road safety, they said. "This rule change also creates a contradictory outcome for the state of Texas," said Tyler Simmons , a Texas Independent Automobile Dealers Association's board member. () " North Texas school district cancels Islamic Games for alleged ties to group governor calls terroristic" Fort Worth Report - Grapevine-Colleyville ISD officials "severed negotiations" with an Islamic group seeking to host an all-ages sporting tournament at a local high school, a district spokesperson confirmed to the Fort Worth Report on Tuesday. The decision came after school administrators were "made aware" that one of the event's sponsors, the Council on American-Islamic Relations, or CAIR, was designated a terrorist organization by Gov. Greg Abbott last year, GCISD spokesperson Nicole Lyons said in an email. But organizers of the Islamic Games said CAIR "has never been, and is not presently, a sponsor, partner, affiliate or supporter" of the sports festival. "We are deeply concerned that a sporting event for children is being targeted," Salaudeen Nausrudeen , president of Islamic Games, said in an email to the Report. "It is our hope that better minds prevail." Islamic Games officials requested to use the Colleyville Heritage High School to host its Dallas chapter's gaming event in May, Lyons said. () 2026 " Jasmine Crockett, James Talarico show different fighting styles in first debate of Senate Democratic primary" Kayla Guo - U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett and state Rep.James Talarico b oth pitched themselves as fighters Saturday during their first, and perhaps only, debate in the Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, with Crockett framing herself as a "street fighter" and Talarico leaning into his record battling GOP proposals in the Texas House. The two Democrats debated for an hour at the Texas AFL-CIO's political convention in Georgetown ahead of the March 3 election. Early voting begins Feb. 17, just over three weeks out from Saturday. Crockett, D-Dallas, and Talarico, D-Austin, are competing for their party's nomination to take on the eventual Republican nominee in November, when Democrats hope that backlash to the Trump administration -- and a GOP nominee damaged from a bruising primary battle -- will help lift the party to its first statewide victory in over three decades. U.S. Sen. John Cornyn , the Republican incumbent, is fighting to defend his seat in a bitter primary against Attorney General Ken Paxton and U.S. Rep. Wesley Hunt of Houston. Crockett has pitched her candidacy, defined by her reputation as a political brawler, as the one best positioned to expand the electorate, with a focus on juicing Democratic turnout and motivating low-propensity voters to show up at the polls. Talarico, meanwhile, has emphasized his Christian faith and a top-versus-bottom brand of economic populism, betting those tenets can win over disaffected Republican and independent voters while also engaging the Democratic base. During the debate, Crockett emphasized her ability to tap into the "rawness of this moment" by taking the fight to Republicans outside Capitol Hill, such as in Minneapolis during the Trump administration's ongoing immigration crackdown. She said this made her best positioned to motivate turnout and win in November. "I am not just sitting in D.C.," she said. "I am getting out into the streets. And right now, to be perfectly honest, if we're going to win and keep our country, we're going to need somebody who can file bills but also knows how to be a street fighter." Talarico, meanwhile, highlighted his experience as a middle school teacher and his record fighting "tooth and nail" in the Texas House, where he frequently went viral over clips of his exchanges with Republicans on proposals including private school vouchers and a mandate to display the Ten Commandments in public school classrooms. "The best indicator of future behavior is past behavior," he said. "I have gone toe to toe with the billionaires who are trying to dismantle public education in this state." The two candidates aligned on most policy issues that came up Saturday, including raising taxes on billionaires, repealing President Donald Trump 's tariffs, banning the sale of offensive weapons to Israel and using congressional authority to check the Trump administration's foreign policy in Venezuela, Greenland and Iran. () " Congressman Wesley Hunt draws scrutiny for missed votes amid campaign for U.S. Senate" Olivia Borgula - U.S. Rep. Wesley Hunt drew renewed criticism for his attendance record on Thursday after narrowly making it to Washington to clinch the GOP's defeat of a resolution that would have blocked further military action in Venezuela. It marked only the fourth time Hunt has cast a vote on the House floor this year, out of 48 votes held in the lower chamber since lawmakers resumed business earlier this month. The absences come as the Houston Republican is waging a primary challenge against U.S. Sen John Cornyn , who has seized on Hunt's spotty voting record with a series of jabs on social media. A Hunt spokesperson defended the absences, saying that no "bills, resolutions, or GOP priorities have been delayed or stopped because of Rep. Hunt's priority of barnstorming Texas to retire John Cornyn." On Friday, responding to what he described as "anyone clutching pearls about missed votes," Hunt added on social media that he has been "crisscrossing Texas nonstop" on the campaign trail. "Anytime you run for higher office, you miss votes. Period," Hunt posted. Speaker Mike Johnson , R-Louisiana, held Thursday's vote open for more than 20 minutes while the chamber awaited Hunt's arrival, prompting shouts from Democrats on the House floor urging Johnson to close the vote. Hunt's 92% rate of missed votes this year has elevated his career mark to 20%, or missing one in five votes since he took office in 2023. Thursday's vote was Hunt's first since Jan. 7. The median percentage of missed votes among current representatives is 2%, according to , a nonprofit website that monitors congressional voting records. Rep.Chip Roy , an Austin Republican who is also running for statewide office in a four-way primary for attorney general, has not missed any votes this month. U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett , D-Dallas, has missed two as she runs opposite Hunt in the Democratic Senate primary. Cornyn, who has missed one vote this year, has repeatedly bashed Hunt on social media over his attendance, while his campaign has launched a website, , to highlight the issue. "Wesley Hunt is refusing to fight for Trump's agenda by putting his selfish political ambitions before his responsibilities," Cornyn campaign spokesperson Matt Mackowiak said in a statement. "He needs to do his job." In the primary, Hunt also faces Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, who has remained quiet on the issue. () " James Talarico unveils Spanish-language TV ad, signaling push for Latino support in Texas Senate bid" Gabby Birenbaum - James Talarico on Wednesday announced a new Spanish-language ad buy, set to air on Univision in nearly a dozen of the state's largest media markets, in what amounts to his first significant push to shore up support from Latino voters as he battles U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett for the Democratic nomination in Texas' U.S. Senate race. The Spanish-language ad, shared first with The Texas Tribune, focuses on Talarico's defense of public education as a teacher and state legislator. It will air twice during Thursday's international friendly soccer match between Mexico and Panama in 11 media markets across the state. Talarico's campaign declined to disclose how much money was behind the ad buy. Titled "¡Vale La Pena!", or "Worth It!", the ad describes Talarico's work in the state House to raise teacher pay and combat private school vouchers. () " New red-leaning congressional seat in North Texas draws wide-open GOP primary field" Olivia Borgula - Last year's redistricting has drawn a flurry of candidates campaigning for Texas' 32nd Congressional District, a seat that will almost certainly go to a Republican under new boundaries that stretch from Dallas into red-leaning East Texas. Of the nine Republicans vying for Rep. Julie Johnson 's seat in the crowded March primary, notable names include Ryan Binkley , a businessman and pastor who self-funded a long-shot presidential bid in 2024; Jace Yarbrough , an attorney, conservative activist and former state Senate candidate; Darrell Day , the district's Republican nominee last cycle; and Paul Bondar , who ran for Oklahoma's 4th District in 2024. Johnson, a freshman Democrat from Farmers Branch, was drawn out of her seat last summer by Republicans in the Texas Legislature, who crafted a new congressional map designed to net up to five seats for the GOP in the 2026 midterms. With her once solidly blue district now virtually out of reach for Democrats, Johnson is running for a nearby seat that contains about a third of her current constituents. The redrawn 32nd District stretches from the Dallas suburbs into rural East Texas. () " Under new postal service guidance, Texans shouldn't wait to send voter registrations or mail-in ballots" Natalia Contreras - Texans seeking to register to vote or cast a ballot by mail may not want to wait until the last minute, thanks to new guidance from the U.S. Postal Service. The USPS last month advised that it may not postmark a piece of mail on the same day that it takes possession of it. Postmarks are applied once mail reaches a processing facility, it said, which may not be the same day it's dropped in a mailbox, for example. The new policy means that even if a voter drops their mail ballot in a box by Election Day, it could be rejected if it's not postmarked on that day. A voter registration application also could miss being postmarked by the Feb. 2 deadline. # _Forget the middle man._

I wanted you to hear this **DIRECTLY** from me!
[RESPOND TO TRUMP](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=60378920be194645b9364f1b3388cee4&_e=UlZroeltl5jk61klODcfpAYbnRV8dK7SHdkAP1EOVoFRfeZ7Ku5CzzNVQRF9DXBP6jRLJ_mwQ29neoVXzCfH5xvpT_IFKNFjn8Sa0IK1SnLS4cxHKeyTjA3UjrJRVosEnxQpMvPU5zBKY8EyICExA7QsTbohaW3ZKe0pOfNn3FPUp5GGH1mKMdSy10Szkdqu8O-teSSz-pNTxYHORxu3oMN23g16Fr6Qz57JtX1RIS8GtWZZZDjqo_4Q-NiRiOyiZ2lmhjIoegVSgCZwjQLPzYcAp-LAPsAzM73NFNfG1f6YgZapJPVfg1UGZyKFm98rDAGEpvGulW7uXpE_KANJM-_PyZI4vtghwJ4cXqnJey8LD3GRXZJ24gSFOX0ABBaUt4a2BmRJte5NXKG0NBPfTnAcPOkMyIDEHPVCTq0KS5PP1wuloMwHbv4KcVmcU7F9Gb6y3PC28ie46Lnh9utGYOvssnuFtifi5x2C53TlRh-Qfvy5ByOppgzQrMYI-BcSpFIklUHBNzCzvpvIDGTsoeK-UZKYRLG8h_Au7i5lvAlMWsPTPnOPMo3CYQjYxj6fDsfGws1aHQhBbuhwXjmj-tbXgFD0vFOh9aYERlFKM2Db4Uue8sRnaFbaIOR2VK6c1sM5a2Ufchuci9LhdLaBFAapo_oM8EA7gBIZsYnDCI3E6hbwAwiT7nELR9L6E1xqdT-wJiiTOEvjk5_BMnK0dOcnkjJjWGgWj0aiG8prW6g%3D)
Thanks to **YOU** , a year ago I was inaugurated as the 47th President of the United States.

We’ve done **SO** much, and it’s truly been a**HELL OF A RIDE**.

Our border is **SHUT** , gas prices have hit a **FIVE-YEAR LOW** , inflation is **UNDER CONTROL** , the economy is **BOOMING** , and the USA is finally **RESPECTED** again.

The Democrats **CAN’T STAND** that the American people are winning again, and they’ll **USE** **EVERY DIRTY TACTIC** possible to destroy our progress.

And make no mistake, Patriot: when they come after me, they’re really coming after **YOU**!

I’m just standing in their way.

We’ve been through **HELL** together, and**NOTHING** has torn our bond apart.
[RESPOND TO TRUMP](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=60378920be194645b9364f1b3388cee4&_e=UlZroeltl5jk61klODcfpAYbnRV8dK7SHdkAP1EOVoFRfeZ7Ku5CzzNVQRF9DXBP6jRLJ_mwQ29neoVXzCfH5xvpT_IFKNFjn8Sa0IK1SnLS4cxHKeyTjA3UjrJRVosEnxQpMvPU5zBKY8EyICExA7QsTbohaW3ZKe0pOfNn3FPUp5GGH1mKMdSy10Szkdqu8O-teSSz-pNTxYHORxu3oMN23g16Fr6Qz57JtX1RIS8GtWZZZDjqo_4Q-NiRiOyiLFhzcMDxArrc7rJT7ZO1do4z7PWeFdgzjOfonjp9rUYliesFAAjDiURCN_qodi3nHTHOtZqN5hHgBfWBsKhP0P02XDEiNFXk1LND5PghUzZUgVEBnceUljO6Vhbd61TW1qW_HvUCVIJld3Hy336LHTps0gWW-acOEJm_ObU21yN7QGP-B5oOcCklaX8XDm-ltsi_2yjmb7OZsjKkRkOgo41EOwXLSToQeJzWKzQOyqPK8H9rfE8LKqYg9vebiPmQqX7ovawKgOr15dHfNv_MtovX1jor9GZ0vDuAt2d89iJMFd4pdJvhWWB-Rgw467XaofNfqIiavZD4hvmtPsQ137EtRUMQZqVqqLz4N2_9fJpuUM1LeHObXyIovlc8KayfyOVuOOmntKef-JzqPB0HI7xkRvDZ-c2ulHvsjhWB5Les0r8mDcAbVScMOTH2F141MdHSlbiExP7bbMBODvwc0xpK-y3dy4Strn05OpDGKOI%3D)
Unlike the Democrats, I **UNDERSTAND** how much you love our country and how hard you work every day to live the American dream.

clean_body: I just stepped out of the Oval Office to send you this | | | | Forget the middle man. I wanted you to hear this DIRECTLY from me! Thanks to YOU , a year ago I was inaugurated as the 47th President of the United States. We’ve done SO much, and it’s truly been aHELL OF A RIDE. Our border is SHUT , gas prices have hit a FIVE-YEAR LOW , inflation is UNDER CONTROL , the economy is BOOMING , and the USA is finally RESPECTED again. The Democrats CAN’T STAND that the American people are winning again, and they’ll USE EVERY DIRTY TACTIC possible to destroy our progress. And make no mistake, Patriot: when they come after me, they’re really coming after YOU! I’m just standing in their way. We’ve been through HELL together, andNOTHING has torn our bond apart. Unlike the Democrats, I UNDERSTAND how much you love our country and how hard you work every day to live the American dream. And I NEVER forget who I’m fighting for: YOU, John! Together, we will finish what we started. Together, we will keep MAKING AMERICA GREAT AGAIN. — President Donald J. Pat

**" Federal appeals court to rehear challenge against Texas' immigration law"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/43801897-53b9-4edb-b4f0-c16f74865a24?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Alejandro Serrano** - Three years ago, Texas lawmakers passed a landmark law that would let state police arrest people suspected of having entered the country illegally by crossing the border.

Such authority has long been the sole responsibility of the federal government, but the state's Republican leaders said Texas, which shares about 1,250 miles of border with Mexico, had a constitutional right to protect its sovereignty as the number of illegal border crossings hit record highs under the Biden administration.

But when a federal appeals court reheard oral arguments Thursday in a legal challenge seeking to stop Texas' law from going into effect, the circumstances outside the courtroom were much different.

The number of people crossing the border has dwindled to a trickle during President **Donald Trump** 's first year back in the White House, setting record lows in a sharp reversal from the influx that inspired Texas' law, known as Senate Bill 4. The new reality on the ground could complicate Texas' legal argument that it has a constitutional right to defend itself against an invasion, like the one state lawmakers claimed was afoot when they enacted the law.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/1d3070c1-fe01-4d98-ae26-e118597195e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Federal court focuses on informant in appeal of Texas death row inmate's conviction"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/a1228ca0-f742-40c4-acb3-a6caae24d48b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Ayden Runnels** - The 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals on Wednesday heard oral arguments once again on the conviction of Texas death row inmate **Brittany Holberg** , which was previously overturned by justices in March.

All 17 of the court's judges listened to arguments and questioned attorneys for both Holberg and the state as they weighed the appeal. Much of the discussion and questions from the judges revolved around **Vickie Kirkpatrick** , who was Holberg's cellmate before her trial.

Holberg was convicted of capital murder in 1998 for killing and robbing 80-year-old **A.B. Towery** , whom she stabbed 58 times and shoved an 11-inch piece of lamp post down his throat while he was still alive. Holberg is one of seven women on death row.

Kirkpatrick became the key witness during trial when she testified that Holberg told her she went to Towery's home to rob him, enjoyed killing Towery and would do so again for drugs or money. Prosecutors did not disclose at trial, however, that Kirkpatrick had been working as a paid informant for the City of Amarillo on several other cases.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/a89e7f71-9568-4827-b3b1-680bc3d8e297?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

* **LOCAL GOVERNMENT**

**" Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar, brother of Rep. Henry Cuellar, indicted on federal fraud charges"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/85286f10-e295-46bd-81fe-83e2bad6447e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Eleanor Klibanoff** - **Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar** appeared before a federal judge Thursday for allegedly using public funds to run a fraudulent for-profit disinfecting business during the COVID-19 pandemic. He has pleaded not guilty and was released on bond.

The South Texas sheriff was indicted alongside his assistant chief in November. The charges, unsealed Thursday, accuse them of opening a private business, Disinfect Pro Master, in April 2020 and entering into agreements with local businesses and restaurants "despite having no employees or supplies of their own."

The company secured a $500,000 contract to clean schools in Laredo, which they fulfilled using department staff and resources, U.S. Attorney **Nicholas Ganjei** of the Southern District of Texas said in a statement.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/e92b0a38-3501-44d1-a763-c68ac73d7534?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Texas counties, auto industry push back on proposed ID restrictions on car registration tied to immigration"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/0788923c-95ea-4c08-b01a-23a6c4437874?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Alex Nguyen** - The Texas Department of Motor Vehicles is facing pushback to its proposed rule changes adding stricter photo identification requirements for vehicle registrations, which would prevent undocumented residents from legally owning vehicles.

The DMV on Wednesday heard public testimonies opposing the plan from around 35 people representing small businesses, industry groups, county tax assessor offices and advocacy organizations from across the state. The proposed changes would not just create more barriers for a broad swath of people to live and work in a car-dependent state, but also harm Texas businesses and road safety, they said.

"This rule change also creates a contradictory outcome for the state of Texas," said **Tyler Simmons** , a Texas Independent Automobile Dealers Association's board member.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/273c52d1-b66f-47e4-83d3-333dfaeb9eba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" North Texas school district cancels Islamic Games for alleged ties to group governor calls terroristic"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/23238027-6849-4231-9cb9-10d95a09235b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Fort Worth Report** - Grapevine-Colleyville ISD officials "severed negotiations" with an Islamic group seeking to host an all-ages sporting tournament at a local high school, a district spokesperson confirmed to the Fort Worth Report on Tuesday.

The decision came after school administrators were "made aware" that one of the event's sponsors, the Council on American-Islamic Relations, or CAIR, was designated a terrorist organization by Gov. Greg Abbott last year, GCISD spokesperson **Nicole Lyons** said in an email.

But organizers of the Islamic Games said CAIR "has never been, and is not presently, a sponsor, partner, affiliate or supporter" of the sports festival.

"We are deeply concerned that a sporting event for children is being targeted," **Salaudeen Nausrudeen** , president of Islamic Games, said in an email to the Report. "It is our hope that better minds prevail."

Islamic Games officials requested to use the Colleyville Heritage High School to host its Dallas chapter's gaming event in May, Lyons said.

* **2026**

**" Jasmine Crockett, James Talarico show different fighting styles in first debate of Senate Democratic primary" **[Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/03b65793-be28-4954-8f02-5309415a3629?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Kayla Guo** - U.S. Rep. **Jasmine Crockett** and state Rep.**James Talarico b** oth pitched themselves as fighters Saturday during their first, and perhaps only, debate in the Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, with Crockett framing herself as a "street fighter" and Talarico leaning into his record battling GOP proposals in the Texas House.

The two Democrats debated for an hour at the Texas AFL-CIO's political convention in Georgetown ahead of the March 3 election. Early voting begins Feb. 17, just over three weeks out from Saturday.

Crockett, D-Dallas, and Talarico, D-Austin, are competing for their party's nomination to take on the eventual Republican nominee in November, when Democrats hope that backlash to the Trump administration -- and a GOP nominee damaged from a bruising primary battle -- will help lift the party to its first statewide victory in over three decades. U.S. Sen. **John Cornyn** , the Republican incumbent, is fighting to defend his seat in a bitter primary against Attorney General **Ken Paxton** and U.S. Rep. **Wesley Hunt** of Houston.

Crockett has pitched her candidacy, defined by her reputation as a political brawler, as the one best positioned to expand the electorate, with a focus on juicing Democratic turnout and motivating low-propensity voters to show up at the polls. Talarico, meanwhile, has emphasized his Christian faith and a top-versus-bottom brand of economic populism, betting those tenets can win over disaffected Republican and independent voters while also engaging the Democratic base.

During the debate, Crockett emphasized her ability to tap into the "rawness of this moment" by taking the fight to Republicans outside Capitol Hill, such as in Minneapolis during the Trump administration's ongoing immigration crackdown. She said this made her best positioned to motivate turnout and win in November.

"I am not just sitting in D.C.," she said. "I am getting out into the streets. And right now, to be perfectly honest, if we're going to win and keep our country, we're going to need somebody who can file bills but also knows how to be a street fighter."

Talarico, meanwhile, highlighted his experience as a middle school teacher and his record fighting "tooth and nail" in the Texas House, where he frequently went viral over clips of his exchanges with Republicans on proposals including private school vouchers and a mandate to display the Ten Commandments in public school classrooms.

"The best indicator of future behavior is past behavior," he said. "I have gone toe to toe with the billionaires who are trying to dismantle public education in this state."

The two candidates aligned on most policy issues that came up Saturday, including raising taxes on billionaires, repealing President **Donald Trump 's** tariffs, banning the sale of offensive weapons to Israel and using congressional authority to check the Trump administration's foreign policy in Venezuela, Greenland and Iran.

(_[Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/03b65793-be28-4954-8f02-5309415a3629?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Congressman Wesley Hunt draws scrutiny for missed votes amid campaign for U.S. Senate"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/6a06f58f-3a29-41cd-adbb-b2ecc7c2a697?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Olivia Borgula** - U.S. Rep. **Wesley Hunt** drew renewed criticism for his attendance record on Thursday after narrowly making it to Washington to clinch the GOP's defeat of a resolution that would have blocked further military action in Venezuela.

It marked only the fourth time Hunt has cast a vote on the House floor this year, out of 48 votes held in the lower chamber since lawmakers resumed business earlier this month. The absences come as the Houston Republican is waging a primary challenge against U.S. Sen **John Cornyn** , who has seized on Hunt's spotty voting record with a series of jabs on social media.

A Hunt spokesperson defended the absences, saying that no "bills, resolutions, or GOP priorities have been delayed or stopped because of Rep. Hunt's priority of barnstorming Texas to retire John Cornyn." On Friday, responding to what he described as "anyone clutching pearls about missed votes," Hunt added on social media that he has been "crisscrossing Texas nonstop" on the campaign trail.

"Anytime you run for higher office, you miss votes. Period," Hunt posted.

Speaker **Mike Johnson** , R-Louisiana, held Thursday's vote open for more than 20 minutes while the chamber awaited Hunt's arrival, prompting shouts from Democrats on the House floor urging Johnson to close the vote.

Hunt's 92% rate of missed votes this year has elevated his career mark to 20%, or missing one in five votes since he took office in 2023. Thursday's vote was Hunt's first since Jan. 7.

The median percentage of missed votes among current representatives is 2%, according to [GovTrack](https://substack.com/redirect/f2fcd2b8-1c7f-454e-a1d2-a6b98e5d59c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a nonprofit website that monitors congressional voting records. Rep.**Chip Roy** , an Austin Republican who is also running for statewide office in a four-way primary for attorney general, has not missed any votes this month. U.S. Rep. **Jasmine Crockett** , D-Dallas, has missed two as she runs opposite Hunt in the Democratic Senate primary.

Cornyn, who has missed one vote this year, has repeatedly bashed Hunt on social media over his attendance, while his campaign has launched a website, [whereswesleyhunt.com](https://substack.com/redirect/10cf11bb-14a6-431e-9038-46869e2cd1fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), to highlight the issue.

"Wesley Hunt is refusing to fight for Trump's agenda by putting his selfish political ambitions before his responsibilities," Cornyn campaign spokesperson **Matt Mackowiak** said in a statement. "He needs to do his job."

In the primary, Hunt also faces Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, who has remained quiet on the issue.

([Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/6a06f58f-3a29-41cd-adbb-b2ecc7c2a697?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))

**" James Talarico unveils Spanish-language TV ad, signaling push for Latino support in Texas Senate bid"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/cfcbfc4b-d6c1-4a18-9710-e13d1106692c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Gabby Birenbaum** - **James Talarico** on Wednesday announced a new Spanish-language ad buy, set to air on Univision in nearly a dozen of the state's largest media markets, in what amounts to his first significant push to shore up support from Latino voters as he battles U.S. Rep. **Jasmine Crockett** for the Democratic nomination in Texas' U.S. Senate race.

The Spanish-language ad, shared first with The Texas Tribune, focuses on Talarico's defense of public education as a teacher and state legislator. It will air twice during Thursday's international friendly soccer match between Mexico and Panama in 11 media markets across the state. Talarico's campaign declined to disclose how much money was behind the ad buy.

Titled "¡Vale La Pena!", or "Worth It!", the ad describes Talarico's work in the state House to raise teacher pay and combat private school vouchers. (_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/b8f664f1-e1f7-44dd-b227-40224704993c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" New red-leaning congressional seat in North Texas draws wide-open GOP primary field"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/826232bf-ca9a-41a6-80c4-70829794d7a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Olivia Borgula** - Last year's redistricting has drawn a flurry of candidates campaigning for Texas' 32nd Congressional District, a seat that will almost certainly go to a Republican under new boundaries that stretch from Dallas into red-leaning East Texas.

Of the nine Republicans vying for Rep. **Julie Johnson** 's seat in the crowded March primary, notable names include **Ryan Binkley** , a businessman and pastor who self-funded a long-shot presidential bid in 2024; **Jace Yarbrough** , an attorney, conservative activist and former state Senate candidate; **Darrell Day** , the district's Republican nominee last cycle; and **Paul Bondar** , who ran for Oklahoma's 4th District in 2024.

Johnson, a freshman Democrat from Farmers Branch, was drawn out of her seat last summer by Republicans in the Texas Legislature, who crafted a new congressional map designed to net up to five seats for the GOP in the 2026 midterms. With her once solidly blue district now virtually out of reach for Democrats, Johnson is running for a nearby seat that contains about a third of her current constituents.

The redrawn 32nd District stretches from the Dallas suburbs into rural East Texas.

(_[TX TRIB](https://substack.com/redirect/91a180ef-2350-4899-abb3-17aa042481d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_)

**" Under new postal service guidance, Texans shouldn't wait to send voter registrations or mail-in ballots"** [Texas Tribune's](https://substack.com/redirect/919868e1-63a8-4b3d-91b0-8e04851ea62c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Natalia Contreras** - Texans seeking to register to vote or cast a ballot by mail may not want to wait until the last minute, thanks to new guidance from the U.S. Postal Service.

The USPS last month advised that it may not postmark a piece of mail on the same day that it takes possession of it. Postmarks are applied once mail reaches a processing facility, it said, which may not be the same day it's dropped in a mailbox, for example.

The new policy means that even if a voter drops their mail ballot in a box by Election Day, it could be rejected if it's not postmarked on that day. A voter registration application also could miss being postmarked by the Feb. 2 deadline.

clean_body: Here's What You Need to Know in Texas Today. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Here's What You Need to Know in Texas Today. | | | Jan 25 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- - BY: THURSDAY 1/22/2026 - FRIDAY 1/23/2026 Good Sunday evening. Thank you for reading as a FREE subscriber. » Become a PAID subscriber for $7/mo or $70/yr . Hope you are staying warm! A WORD FROM SENATOR PHIL GRAMM ABOUT MUST READ TEXAS " If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | | ---|---|--- Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . TOP NEWS " An arctic storm is expected to blanket the state. Here's what Texans can expect." Colleen DeGuzman - Texans are bracing for a harsh winter storm that's expected to plunge the entire state into subfreezing temperatures starting Friday, bringing an arctic mix of snow, sleet and freezing rain. Frigid air is blowing in from northern Canada, and according to National Weather Service Meteorologist Kurt Van Speybroeck , it's expected to reach the very southern tip of Texas by Sunday morning. Texans from Wichita Falls west to Lubbock and Amarillo should be preparing for snowy weather late Friday. The cold air and precipitation is expected to move south Saturday, and by Sunday morning, residents in Brownsville should expect temperatures to dip below freezing. Every part of the state, from El Paso to the Houston region, will experience a hard freeze during the storm, Speybroeck said. "By Sunday, the whole state will definitely be experiencing well-below normal temperatures and dangerous wind chills," Speybroeck said. () " Federal appeals court to rehear challenge against Texas' immigration law" Alejandro Serrano - Three years ago, Texas lawmakers passed a landmark law that would let state police arrest people suspected of having entered the country illegally by crossing the border. Such authority has long been the sole responsibility of the federal government, but the state's Republican leaders said Texas, which shares about 1,250 miles of border with Mexico, had a constitutional right to protect its sovereignty as the number of illegal border crossings hit record highs under the Biden administration. But when a federal appeals court reheard oral arguments Thursday in a legal challenge seeking to stop Texas' law from going into effect, the circumstances outside the courtroom were much different. The number of people crossing the border has dwindled to a trickle during President Donald Trump 's first year back in the White House, setting record lows in a sharp reversal from the influx that inspired Texas' law, known as Senate Bill 4. The new reality on the ground could complicate Texas' legal argument that it has a constitutional right to defend itself against an invasion, like the one state lawmakers claimed was afoot when they enacted the law. () " Federal court focuses on informant in appeal of Texas death row inmate's conviction" Ayden Runnels - The 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals on Wednesday heard oral arguments once again on the conviction of Texas death row inmate Brittany Holberg , which was previously overturned by justices in March. All 17 of the court's judges listened to arguments and questioned attorneys for both Holberg and the state as they weighed the appeal. Much of the discussion and questions from the judges revolved around Vickie Kirkpatrick , who was Holberg's cellmate before her trial. Holberg was convicted of capital murder in 1998 for killing and robbing 80-year-old A.B. Towery , whom she stabbed 58 times and shoved an 11-inch piece of lamp post down his throat while he was still alive. Holberg is one of seven women on death row. Kirkpatrick became the key witness during trial when she testified that Holberg told her she went to Towery's home to rob him, enjoyed killing Towery and would do so again for drugs or money. Prosecutors did not disclose at trial, however, that Kirkpatrick had been working as a paid informant for the City of Amarillo on several other cases. () LOCAL GOVERNMENT " Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar, brother of Rep. Henry Cuellar, indicted on federal fraud charges" Eleanor Klibanoff - Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar appeared before a federal judge Thursday for allegedly using public funds to run a fraudulent for-profit disinfecting business during the COVID-19 pandemic. He has pleaded not guilty and was released on bond. The South Texas sheriff was indicted alongside his assistant chief in November. The charges, unsealed Thursday, accuse them of opening a private business, Disinfect Pro Master, in April 2020 and entering into agreements with local businesses and restaurants "despite having no employees or supplies of their own." The company secured a $500,000 contract to clean schools in Laredo, which they fulfilled using department staff and resources, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei of the Southern District of Texas said in a statement. () " Texas counties, auto industry push back on proposed ID restrictions on car registration tied to immigration" Alex Nguyen - The Texas Department of Motor Vehicles is facing pushback to its proposed rule changes adding stricter photo identification requirements for vehicle registrations, which would prevent undocumented residents from legally owning vehicles. The DMV on Wednesday heard public testimonies opposing the plan from around 35 people representing small businesses, industry groups, county tax assessor offices and advocacy organizations from across the state. The proposed changes would not just create more barriers for a broad swath of people to live and work in a car-dependent state, but also harm Texas businesses and road safety, they said. "This rule change also creates a contradictory outcome for the state of Texas," said Tyler Simmons , a Texas Independent Automobile Dealers Association's board member. () " North Texas school district cancels Islamic Games for alleged ties to group governor calls terroristic" Fort Worth Report - Grapevine-Colleyville ISD officials "severed negotiations" with an Islamic group seeking to host an all-ages sporting tournament at a local high school, a district spokesperson confirmed to the Fort Worth Report on Tuesday. The decision came after school administrators were "made aware" that one of the event's sponsors, the Council on American-Islamic Relations, or CAIR, was designated a terrorist organization by Gov. Greg Abbott last year, GCISD spokesperson Nicole Lyons said in an email. But organizers of the Islamic Games said CAIR "has never been, and is not presently, a sponsor, partner, affiliate or supporter" of the sports festival. "We are deeply concerned that a sporting event for children is being targeted," Salaudeen Nausrudeen , president of Islamic Games, said in an email to the Report. "It is our hope that better minds prevail." Islamic Games officials requested to use the Colleyville Heritage High School to host its Dallas chapter's gaming event in May, Lyons said. () 2026 " Jasmine Crockett, James Talarico show different fighting styles in first debate of Senate Democratic primary" Kayla Guo - U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett and state Rep.James Talarico b oth pitched themselves as fighters Saturday during their first, and perhaps only, debate in the Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, with Crockett framing herself as a "street fighter" and Talarico leaning into his record battling GOP proposals in the Texas House. The two Democrats debated for an hour at the Texas AFL-CIO's political convention in Georgetown ahead of the March 3 election. Early voting begins Feb. 17, just over three weeks out from Saturday. Crockett, D-Dallas, and Talarico, D-Austin, are competing for their party's nomination to take on the eventual Republican nominee in November, when Democrats hope that backlash to the Trump administration -- and a GOP nominee damaged from a bruising primary battle -- will help lift the party to its first statewide victory in over three decades. U.S. Sen. John Cornyn , the Republican incumbent, is fighting to defend his seat in a bitter primary against Attorney General Ken Paxton and U.S. Rep. Wesley Hunt of Houston. Crockett has pitched her candidacy, defined by her reputation as a political brawler, as the one best positioned to expand the electorate, with a focus on juicing Democratic turnout and motivating low-propensity voters to show up at the polls. Talarico, meanwhile, has emphasized his Christian faith and a top-versus-bottom brand of economic populism, betting those tenets can win over disaffected Republican and independent voters while also engaging the Democratic base. During the debate, Crockett emphasized her ability to tap into the "rawness of this moment" by taking the fight to Republicans outside Capitol Hill, such as in Minneapolis during the Trump administration's ongoing immigration crackdown. She said this made her best positioned to motivate turnout and win in November. "I am not just sitting in D.C.," she said. "I am getting out into the streets. And right now, to be perfectly honest, if we're going to win and keep our country, we're going to need somebody who can file bills but also knows how to be a street fighter." Talarico, meanwhile, highlighted his experience as a middle school teacher and his record fighting "tooth and nail" in the Texas House, where he frequently went viral over clips of his exchanges with Republicans on proposals including private school vouchers and a mandate to display the Ten Commandments in public school classrooms. "The best indicator of future behavior is past behavior," he said. "I have gone toe to toe with the billionaires who are trying to dismantle public education in this state." The two candidates aligned on most policy issues that came up Saturday, including raising taxes on billionaires, repealing President Donald Trump 's tariffs, banning the sale of offensive weapons to Israel and using congressional authority to check the Trump administration's foreign policy in Venezuela, Greenland and Iran. () " Congressman Wesley Hunt draws scrutiny for missed votes amid campaign for U.S. Senate" Olivia Borgula - U.S. Rep. Wesley Hunt drew renewed criticism for his attendance record on Thursday after narrowly making it to Washington to clinch the GOP's defeat of a resolution that would have blocked further military action in Venezuela. It marked only the fourth time Hunt has cast a vote on the House floor this year, out of 48 votes held in the lower chamber since lawmakers resumed business earlier this month. The absences come as the Houston Republican is waging a primary challenge against U.S. Sen John Cornyn , who has seized on Hunt's spotty voting record with a series of jabs on social media. A Hunt spokesperson defended the absences, saying that no "bills, resolutions, or GOP priorities have been delayed or stopped because of Rep. Hunt's priority of barnstorming Texas to retire John Cornyn." On Friday, responding to what he described as "anyone clutching pearls about missed votes," Hunt added on social media that he has been "crisscrossing Texas nonstop" on the campaign trail. "Anytime you run for higher office, you miss votes. Period," Hunt posted. Speaker Mike Johnson , R-Louisiana, held Thursday's vote open for more than 20 minutes while the chamber awaited Hunt's arrival, prompting shouts from Democrats on the House floor urging Johnson to close the vote. Hunt's 92% rate of missed votes this year has elevated his career mark to 20%, or missing one in five votes since he took office in 2023. Thursday's vote was Hunt's first since Jan. 7. The median percentage of missed votes among current representatives is 2%, according to , a nonprofit website that monitors congressional voting records. Rep.Chip Roy , an Austin Republican who is also running for statewide office in a four-way primary for attorney general, has not missed any votes this month. U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett , D-Dallas, has missed two as she runs opposite Hunt in the Democratic Senate primary. Cornyn, who has missed one vote this year, has repeatedly bashed Hunt on social media over his attendance, while his campaign has launched a website, , to highlight the issue. "Wesley Hunt is refusing to fight for Trump's agenda by putting his selfish political ambitions before his responsibilities," Cornyn campaign spokesperson Matt Mackowiak said in a statement. "He needs to do his job." In the primary, Hunt also faces Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, who has remained quiet on the issue. () " James Talarico unveils Spanish-language TV ad, signaling push for Latino support in Texas Senate bid" Gabby Birenbaum - James Talarico on Wednesday announced a new Spanish-language ad buy, set to air on Univision in nearly a dozen of the state's largest media markets, in what amounts to his first significant push to shore up support from Latino voters as he battles U.S. Rep. Jasmine Crockett for the Democratic nomination in Texas' U.S. Senate race. The Spanish-language ad, shared first with The Texas Tribune, focuses on Talarico's defense of public education as a teacher and state legislator. It will air twice during Thursday's international friendly soccer match between Mexico and Panama in 11 media markets across the state. Talarico's campaign declined to disclose how much money was behind the ad buy. Titled "¡Vale La Pena!", or "Worth It!", the ad describes Talarico's work in the state House to raise teacher pay and combat private school vouchers. () " New red-leaning congressional seat in North Texas draws wide-open GOP primary field" Olivia Borgula - Last year's redistricting has drawn a flurry of candidates campaigning for Texas' 32nd Congressional District, a seat that will almost certainly go to a Republican under new boundaries that stretch from Dallas into red-leaning East Texas. Of the nine Republicans vying for Rep. Julie Johnson 's seat in the crowded March primary, notable names include Ryan Binkley , a businessman and pastor who self-funded a long-shot presidential bid in 2024; Jace Yarbrough , an attorney, conservative activist and former state Senate candidate; Darrell Day , the district's Republican nominee last cycle; and Paul Bondar , who ran for Oklahoma's 4th District in 2024. Johnson, a freshman Democrat from Farmers Branch, was drawn out of her seat last summer by Republicans in the Texas Legislature, who crafted a new congressional map designed to net up to five seats for the GOP in the 2026 midterms. With her once solidly blue district now virtually out of reach for Democrats, Johnson is running for a nearby seat that contains about a third of her current constituents. The redrawn 32nd District stretches from the Dallas suburbs into rural East Texas. () " Under new postal service guidance, Texans shouldn't wait to send voter registrations or mail-in ballots" Natalia Contreras - Texans seeking to register to vote or cast a ballot by mail may not want to wait until the last minute, thanks to new guidance from the U.S. Postal Service. The USPS last month advised that it may not postmark a piece of mail on the same day that it takes possession of it. Postmarks are applied once mail reaches a processing facility, it said, which may not be the same day it's dropped in a mailbox, for example. The new policy means that even if a voter drops their mail ballot in a box by Election Day, it could be rejected if it's not postmarked on that day. A voter registration application also could miss being postmarked by the Feb. 2 deadline. **“Federal appeals court to rehear challenge against Texas’ immigration law”** [Texas Tribune’s](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/v1/c/rr%2Fajktlry%2FhQv3wuh0ZRz4ETiZLN28GPN3EutmKwruEeGNqznngk9X8%2FT%2F5%0AbVYsoxsfXNkOuTf62eBRpLHc3pK6%2B3TlIgSKeMPsK%2B8tiELcTrPb5TefDOx%2B%0AgLQM%2BxNYGLAxo16BoulEAuUdlzxAR%2B92MMNwOE0RlhciNfe5SzI%3D%0A/13b098ff04d59fa4) **Alejandro Serrano** – Three years ago, Texas lawmakers passed a landmark law that would let state police arrest people suspected of having entered the country illegally by crossing the border.

Such authority has long been the sole responsibility of the federal government, but the state’s Republican leaders said Texas, which shares about 1,250 miles of border with Mexico, had a constitutional right to protect its sovereignty as the number of illegal border crossings hit record highs under the Biden administration.

But when a federal appeals court reheard oral arguments Thursday in a legal challenge seeking to stop Texas’ law from going into effect, the circumstances outside the courtroom were much different.

The number of people crossing the border has dwindled to a trickle during President **Donald Trump** ’s first year back in the White House, setting record lows in a sharp reversal from the influx that inspired Texas’ law, known as Senate Bill 4. The new reality on the ground could complicate Texas’ legal argument that it has a constitutional right to defend itself against an invasion, like the one state lawmakers claimed was afoot when they enacted the law.

**“Federal court focuses on informant in appeal of Texas death row inmate’s conviction”** [Texas Tribune’s](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/v1/c/Gpgsih39e179%2BVN3bx33fICneAmplyHwgVOSJLhHKnvmgWWhmecpdZDW4VoK%0AMCkdEhaR7ZWhVxSG8hf3bqmbRwUTcRlxo4bIasYT%2FNOC3uTn8InXY%2BdtyinA%0AHRbcHUOe%2BbfToUvYkIIxPCQPhbwfNn0uVKZydWsYsdkra3EGdso%3D%0A/995e74ea8753a8b6) **Ayden Runnels** – The 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals on Wednesday heard oral arguments once again on the conviction of Texas death row inmate **Brittany Holberg** , which was previously overturned by justices in March.

All 17 of the court’s judges listened to arguments and questioned attorneys for both Holberg and the state as they weighed the appeal. Much of the discussion and questions from the judges revolved around **Vickie Kirkpatrick** , who was Holberg’s cellmate before her trial.

Holberg was convicted of capital murder in 1998 for killing and robbing 80-year-old **A.B. Towery** , whom she stabbed 58 times and shoved an 11-inch piece of lamp post down his throat while he was still alive. Holberg is one of seven women on death row.

Kirkpatrick became the key witness during trial when she testified that Holberg told her she went to Towery’s home to rob him, enjoyed killing Towery and would do so again for drugs or money. Prosecutors did not disclose at trial, however, that Kirkpatrick had been working as a paid informant for the City of Amarillo on several other cases.

* **LOCAL GOVERNMENT**

**“Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar, brother of Rep. Henry Cuellar, indicted on federal fraud charges”** [Texas Tribune’s](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/v1/c/9l9YhfP3uQSigODFU9WcDgNT1%2FwjvVlFNS%2BL3OdUATsABD8sbIXUraTgL49m%0A9gplVFERnIxOkWcHIbfWjROawwKfowfa2N%2B0PqAzGbEZ6VKUNcP8YGuf%2Fy84%0AhihI6cyyySOKUVpYxFhXyRQKL7o6d0WEXq1d68nA%2F8W%2FP2OAsIo%3D%0A/9dd45a7d86ccb2a8) **Eleanor Klibanoff** – **Webb County Sheriff Martin Cuellar** appeared before a federal judge Thursday for allegedly using public funds to run a fraudulent for-profit disinfecting business during the COVID-19 pandemic. He has pleaded not guilty and was released on bond.

The South Texas sheriff was indicted alongside his assistant chief in November. The charges, unsealed Thursday, accuse them of opening a private business, Disinfect Pro Master, in April 2020 and entering into agreements with local businesses and restaurants “despite having no employees or supplies of their own.”

The company secured a $500,000 contract to clean schools in Laredo, which they fulfilled using department staff and resources, U.S. Attorney **Nicholas Ganjei** of the Southern District of Texas said in a statement.

**“Texas counties, auto industry push back on proposed ID restrictions on car registration tied to immigration”** [Texas Tribune’s](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/v1/c/KuX3tF0xT70oRJFdEX5H%2BvEhFdEryfT80IKSO0wr37dJtMXV3rs8yRItYgQr%0ATWUfIlWPZhj8ib3VD%2Fjt%2B3TRfI4%2BoA87qek8RLFxwvpbpnc3PedcYdd%2BY%2F2H%0A%2Fy7RWjy8uNU7BoEbm0Uyg7R8csxBN4Eo3I2UAim4hQ%2Fa8mmSDUI%3D%0A/5e0c551b91a1177f) **Alex Nguyen** – The Texas Department of Motor Vehicles is facing pushback to its proposed rule changes adding stricter photo identification requirements for vehicle registrations, which would prevent undocumented residents from legally owning vehicles.

The DMV on Wednesday heard public testimonies opposing the plan from around 35 people representing small businesses, industry groups, county tax assessor offices and advocacy organizations from across the state. The proposed changes would not just create more barriers for a broad swath of people to live and work in a car-dependent state, but also harm Texas businesses and road safety, they said.

“This rule change also creates a contradictory outcome for the state of Texas,” said **Tyler Simmons** , a Texas Independent Automobile Dealers Association’s board member.
